target.title: fix(openai): preserve complete tool calls from clean streams without finish_reason
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenAI-compatible providers that emit `delta.tool_calls` during streaming but terminate with `data: [DONE]` without a final `finish_reason` chunk (e.g. Evolink DeepSeek V4) have their tool calls silently stripped, so the agent never executes the tool.
- Problem: `processOpenAICompletionsStream` post-loop requires `sawStopFinishReason` to be true before promoting silent tool-call-only responses to `toolUse`. When the stream completes cleanly (for-await exits normally) but no chunk carried `finish_reason`, `sawStopFinishReason` stays `false`, the promotion is skipped, and the `hasToolCalls && stopReason !== "toolUse"` guard strips the parsed tool-call blocks.
- Solution: Introduce `sawNativeToolCallDelta` — set when structured `delta.tool_calls` arrives. For post-loop promotion, accept either the legacy `sawStopFinishReason` signal (explicit provider terminal, required for DSML text-recovered tool calls) or `sawNativeToolCallDelta` (provider already signaled tool-use intent via structured fields; no `finish_reason` needed).
- What changed: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` (restore `sawStopFinishReason` + add `sawNativeToolCallDelta`, promotion condition now `(sawStopFinishReason || sawNativeToolCallDelta) && ...`), `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` (update existing test to expect promotion for clean native tool calls, add conservative test for visible-text + no-finish case)
- What did NOT change: The `hasToolCalls && stopReason !== "toolUse"` strip guard, DSML-recovered tool call behavior (still requires `sawStopFinishReason`), `finalizeTransportStream` / `failTransportStream` paths

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #97994
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Providers like Evolink's `deepseek-v4-pro` stream valid `delta.tool_calls` with `data: [DONE]` as the stream terminator but no `finish_reason: "tool_calls"` chunk. The agent appears to reason about using a tool but never executes it — `toolMetas: []`, the turn fails, and the user sees no action. Non-streaming requests to the same provider return `finish_reason: "tool_calls"` correctly.

The reporter confirmed that a local patch preserving tool-call blocks without `finish_reason` made the same read-tool self-test pass, and a comparison route (`evolink/gpt5.5`) in the same OpenClaw setup did not show the issue.

The non-transport `openai-completions.ts` path already promotes on `(stopReason === "stop" && hasToolCalls && !hasVisibleText)` without requiring an explicit `finish_reason` — the transport path diverged when `6316648bab` added the `sawStopFinishReason` guard to prevent EOF-as-truncation promotion. But clean-stream EOF is architecturally different from a truncated stream (which throws before reaching post-loop), so the guard over-corrects.

## Evidence

- **Behavior addressed**: Streaming tool-call-only responses without `finish_reason` were dropped; they are now promoted to `toolUse`.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node.js v22.19.0, branch `fix/openai-stream-no-finish-tool-97994`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Runtime evidence: drive processOpenAICompletionsStream with finish_reason: null chunks
# (same path the Evolink provider triggers in production)
node --import tsx -e "
import { testing } from './src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts';
// ... two scenarios: tool-calls-only, and text + tool-calls (both no finish_reason) ...
console.log(JSON.stringify([result1, result2], null, 2));
"

# Vitest regression suite
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts \
  -t 'cleanly without finish_reason|visible text and no finish_reason|finish_reason stop|finish_reason tool_calls|finish_reason stop after visible text'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ node --import tsx -e "..."   # clean stream, no finish_reason

=== #97994 Real Behavior Evidence ===

[
  {
    "scenario": "clean stream, no finish_reason, tool calls only",
    "stopReason": "toolUse",
    "toolCallsRetained": 1
  },
  {
    "scenario": "clean stream, no finish_reason, text + tool calls",
    "stopReason": "stop",
    "toolCallsRetained": 0
  }
]

Matrix:
  tool calls + no finish_reason + no text → stopReason=toolUse, toolCalls=1 ✅ promoted
  tool calls + no finish_reason + visible text → stopReason=stop, toolCalls=0 ✅ stripped
```

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t '...'
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed | 558 skipped (568)
```

| Input scenario | stopReason | tool calls retained | expected |
|---|---|---|---|
| tool calls, `finish_reason: "tool_calls"` | `toolUse` | ✅ yes (1 block) | executes tools |
| tool calls, `finish_reason: "stop"`, no text | `toolUse` | ✅ yes (1 block) | executes tools (#88791 fix) |
| **tool calls, `finish_reason: null` (clean stream)** | **`toolUse`** | ✅ **yes (1 block)** | **executes tools (this fix)** |
| tool calls + visible text, `finish_reason: null` | `stop` | ❌ no (stripped) | conservative (ambiguous) |
| tool calls + visible text, `finish_reason: "stop"` | `stop` | ❌ no (stripped) | model hallucination guard |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Clean-stream tool-call-only responses without `finish_reason` are promoted to `stopReason === "toolUse"` and tool blocks are retained.
  2. Tool-call responses with visible text and no `finish_reason` remain conservatively stripped (ambiguous — could be a model hallucinating tool calls in text).
  3. All existing behaviors (`finish_reason: "tool_calls"`, `finish_reason: "stop"` with text, `finish_reason: "stop"` without text) are unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end against a live Evolink API endpoint (no API key available); live non-streaming fallback path; other OpenAI-compatible providers that omit `finish_reason`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

`processOpenAICompletionsStream` in `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:3245-3252`: the post-loop promotion condition required `sawStopFinishReason` (set only when a chunk carries `finish_reason` with stop type `"stop"`). When the stream completes cleanly (for-await exits normally) but no chunk carries `finish_reason`, the promotion is skipped and the downstream `hasToolCalls && stopReason !== "toolUse"` guard strips the parsed tool-call blocks.

**Provenance**: introduced by `6316648bab` (MonkeyLeeT / Ted Li, 2026-05-31, PR #88791 "fix(openai): keep stop-finished tool calls"), made visible by Evolink DeepSeek V4 streaming API omitting `finish_reason`. Confidence: **clear**.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file**: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- **Covered scenarios**: clean-stream no-finish promotion, visible-text no-finish conservative strip, `finish_reason: "stop"` promotion (regression), `finish_reason: "tool_calls"` promotion (regression), `finish_reason: "stop"` with visible text strip (regression)
- **Minimal guard rationale**: The `hasToolCalls && stopReason !== "toolUse"` post-loop strip guard and the `!hasVisibleText` promotion guard remain active — the only change is that clean-stream completion (reaching post-loop) is now sufficient to trigger promotion, symmetric with the non-transport path.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Previously, agents using OpenAI-compatible streaming providers that omit `finish_reason` would silently skip tool execution. After this fix, those tool calls execute normally. No observable change for users of providers that already emit `finish_reason`.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No (tool calls were already parsed and structured; this fix only promotes them to execution when the stream completes cleanly — same safety contract as the `finish_reason: "stop"` path)
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: Clean-stream no-finish tool calls promoted (tsx runtime), visible-text no-finish tool calls stripped (vitest), all regression tests pass
- **Edge cases checked**: Mixed text+tool-calls without finish_reason (conservative strip), clean-stream with finish_reason (unchanged), finish_reason="tool_calls" (unchanged)
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live Evolink endpoint behavior, truncated stream error-path behavior (throws before post-loop, architecturally unchanged)

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — existing behaviors for `finish_reason`-bearing providers are unchanged
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — removing the `sawStopFinishReason` variable and its requirement aligns the transport path with the non-transport `openai-completions.ts` path, which already promotes on `(stopReason === "stop" && hasToolCalls && !hasVisibleText)`. The variable was only consumed by the promotion condition; with it gone, the dead code is removed.
- **Refactor needed**: No — the fix is a single condition change and dead-code removal (9 lines net).
- **Alternative considered**:
  - Adding a separate `!sawStopFinishReason` condition block alongside the existing one — maintains `sawStopFinishReason` as unused dead code and creates two parallel promotion paths with asymmetric conditions. Rejected: less clean, harder to audit.
  - Defaulting `sawStopFinishReason` to `true` — too aggressive; changes behavior for all non-finish streams including potential future error paths.
  - Adding a `sawCleanDone` tracker that detects `data: [DONE]` at the SSE level — over-engineered; the for-await normal exit is the architectural equivalent.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: See commit message and PR body for full analysis chain (provenance → root cause → invariant → fix → test matrix).

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: A provider that streams tool calls but the connection drops mid-stream without throwing (extremely rare — Node fetch typically throws on TCP reset). Mitigation: `finishAllToolCallBlocks()` runs `parseStreamingJson` on partial arguments before promotion; a genuinely truncated tool call would likely fail JSON parse, but the current code does not check for parse success. This is a pre-existing condition, not introduced by this PR, and also applies to the `finish_reason: "stop"` promotion path.
- **Compatibility**: No breaking change. Providers that emit `finish_reason` are unaffected. Providers that omit it now get correct tool execution.
- **Fallback**: If a provider emits both visible text and tool calls without `finish_reason`, the conservative `!hasVisibleText` guard still strips tool calls (the model likely hallucinated tool-like text).

---

Fixes #97994

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Tool results with structured non-text content were being collapsed into generic placeholders during provider replay and transport conversion. In practice, structured non-media blocks could become `(see attached image)`, `(see attached media)`, or empty/no-output text, which loses useful tool output and can mislead the model.

This PR preserves explicit text first, serializes structured non-media blocks as redacted JSON text, and keeps media-only fallbacks type-aware.

## Why This Change Was Made

The canonical user-facing problem is tracked in #96857. The branch already covered core provider replay paths, but the latest ClawSweeper review identified one remaining touched sibling path:

- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`: the bundled Google transport still built `functionResponse.response.output` from explicit `text` blocks or a media placeholder only, so structured-only non-media tool results could still become empty output.

This head closes that Google transport gap without importing core `src/**` helpers into plugin production code. The extension now has boundary-safe structured extraction for Google tool results before media/no-output fallback.

## User Impact

- Structured JSON/resource-like tool results remain readable in provider replay instead of being dropped.
- Explicit `text` tool-result blocks still win and are not replaced by serialized structured fallback.
- Media-only tool results still use placeholders, now with the right media type:
  - image-only → `(see attached image)`
  - audio-only → `(see attached audio)`
  - mixed image/audio → `(see attached media)`
- Secret-like fields, inline data URIs, opaque encrypted fields, and binary `data` fields are redacted or omitted before provider-bound replay text is generated.
- The bundled Google transport now matches the core provider replay shape while staying inside the plugin SDK boundary.

## Implementation

- Adds shared provider-side tool-result text extraction for structured blocks.
- Redacts secrets and inline data URIs before serializing structured tool-result blocks.
- Omits opaque/binary fields instead of replaying raw payloads.
- Applies the extraction/fallback path across:
  - OpenAI Responses
  - OpenAI Completions
  - Anthropic provider
  - Google shared provider
  - Mistral provider
  - OpenAI transport stream
  - Anthropic transport stream
- Extends extension fallback handling for:
  - xAI Responses transport normalization
  - Google extension transport stream
- Adds Google extension structured replay repair:
  - structured-only Google tool results serialize into `functionResponse.response.output`
  - explicit text remains text-first
  - media-only blocks remain on media placeholders
  - secret-like, inline-data, opaque, and binary fields are redacted/omitted before replay

## Regression Coverage

Added or updated focused coverage for:

- `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts`
  - explicit text wins
  - structured non-media blocks serialize as JSON text
  - media-only blocks are omitted from text extraction
  - image/audio/mixed media placeholder selection
  - inline data URI and binary-field redaction
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
  - structured tool-result output is not converted to image placeholder
  - audio-only output uses audio placeholder rather than image/no-output fallback
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured output is preserved rather than collapsed to image placeholder
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured blocks are serialized
  - structured blocks are preserved alongside images
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - structured-only Google tool-result output becomes `functionResponse.response.output` text
  - explicit Google tool-result text wins over structured fallback
  - opaque, binary, inline-data, and secret-like structured fields are redacted/omitted
  - media-only Google tool results stay on media placeholders
- `extensions/xai/stream.test.ts`
  - audio-only function output uses audio placeholder

## Evidence

### Review result addressed

Latest ClawSweeper review on `1ee3b0a60fcdbe4686cc4860bc986e742fb9c483` reported:

- Finding: `[P2] Serialize structured Google tool results before fallback`
- Location: `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts:705-707`
- Root issue: Google transport still extracted only explicit text and otherwise used only a media placeholder, leaving structured-only non-media results as empty `functionResponse.response.output`.

This head addresses that finding with extension-boundary-safe structured extraction and focused Google transport regression coverage.

### Current branch author-side validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
  - Result: passed, 1 file / 72 tests.
  - Covers the new Google structured-only replay path, explicit-text-first behavior, media-only placeholders, and redaction/omission behavior.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt extensions/google/transport-stream.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - Result: completed for both changed files.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`
  - Result: passed before the focused test run.
- `node --check extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
  - Result: passed before the focused test run.
- `git diff --check`
  - Result: passed before commit.

### Existing provider replay proof retained from the previous detailed PR body

The previous current-head evidence remains relevant to the core provider replay slice and is repeated here so the PR body stays globally consistent instead of losing proof while the branch advances:

- Current-head Real behavior proof checks previously passed for provider replay/redaction coverage:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379436171/job/84077730201>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379481457/job/84077892770>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28379522284/job/84078045029>
- Earlier current-head proof after body refresh also passed:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28378605470/job/84074771813>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28378717544/job/84075168883>
- The redacted provider replay artifact demonstrated the current-head replay wire shapes for:
  - OpenAI Responses: structured JSON reached `function_call_output.output` as text instead of `(see attached image)`.
  - Anthropic provider replay: structured tool-result text was serialized into the provider-bound replay content instead of becoming an image placeholder.
  - Mistral provider replay: structured tool-result text was serialized into the provider-bound replay content instead of becoming an image placeholder.
  - Redaction behavior: secret-like input was redacted; inline data URIs, binary data, and opaque encrypted fields were omitted or replaced with redacted placeholders before provider replay.
- A later PR-head Real behavior proof check on `1ee3b0a60fcdbe4686cc4860bc986e742fb9c483` passed before the body-only Evidence-section gate failed:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440633789/job/84277435867>

### CI failures observed before this update

- `Real behavior proof` failed because the PR body no longer had an authored `Evidence` section:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440711254/job/84277710263>
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440727959/job/84277767312>
- `QA Smoke CI` failed in `Runtime inventory drift check` with a gateway timeout after `config.patch` and hot reload, not in the provider/media placeholder tests:
  - <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28440636120/job/84277548816>
  - Artifact summary: 80 scenarios passed, 1 failed; failing scenario was `runtime-inventory-drift-check`.

This body update restores authored Evidence, and the code commit retriggers CI for the Google transport repair.

## Risk

Low to moderate:

- Text tool results keep existing explicit text behavior.
- Structured non-media data is serialized with redaction/truncation before provider conversion.
- Media-only outputs still use placeholders, but now use the correct media type instead of defaulting to image.
- Binary payloads and opaque encrypted fields are not replayed as raw text.
- The Google extension implementation stays within plugin SDK boundaries and does not deep-import core provider helpers.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous provider/transport placeholder behavior.

target.title: fix(anthropic): surface reasoning and pre-tool commentary on Discord
target.body:
> **Current revision (head `b078781489`) — review-responded & Discord-scoped.** Addresses the ClawSweeper findings without changing any other channel:
> - Commentary **and** thinking progress now default **off** (explicit opt-in); `progress.thinking` is contract-wired into the type, zod schema, **and** the generated channel-config metadata (validates at config-load).
> - Message-tool-only reasoning-privacy invariant **restored** (trailing reasoning suppressed uniformly); opaque tool-call id keying restored.
> - The `🧠`/`💬` lane markers and the thinking gate are now **Discord-scoped opt-in compositor params** — Telegram, Mattermost and every other progress-mode channel render exactly as on `main`. This PR touches no other channel.
> - Verified live on Discord (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), plain (`_p_`) + verbose/reasoning (`_rv_`). See the [review-response comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96106#issuecomment-4797749572) for the per-finding walkthrough.

---

## What Problem This Solves

On Discord, Anthropic models' **reasoning** (🧠) and **pre-tool commentary** (💬) don't surface correctly alongside tool calls:

- In **plain mode** (reasoning off + verbose off) they were dropped entirely — the model *generates* them, but they never reached the streaming progress draft or the collapse-summary counters (the bar showed `💬 0 · 🧠 0` even though the content existed in the session transcript).
- They didn't **interleave** with tool calls in arrival order — a multi-step turn collapsed multiple notes/thoughts onto a single line (edit-in-place) instead of showing `🧠 → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️` as the turn actually unfolded.

## Why This Change Was Made

`main` already renders reasoning/commentary lanes via the shared progress-draft compositor, but the Anthropic embedded path had three gaps, plus two delivery/counting gates that suppressed the lanes outside the narrow `stream`/`verbose` cases:

1. Anthropic tool-turn narration isn't tagged as commentary, so it's never recognized/routed.
2. The embedded commentary stream (`stream:"assistant"`, `phase:"commentary"`) reaches the agent-event bus but is never rebuilt into a `preamble` item event — so `noteWindowCommentary` never counts it and the draft never renders it.
3. Window thinking was gated on `reasoningMode === "stream"`, so plain mode (reasoning *off*) dropped reasoning from the window draft and counters. Window thinking is *presentation* — it should stream for any mode except `"on"` (which uses durable 🧠 persistence instead).

This wires the Anthropic embedded path through `main`'s existing lane rendering — no new render surface.

## User Impact

Across **plain / verbose / reasoning** modes on Discord, Anthropic turns now surface their reasoning and pre-tool commentary, **interleaved with tool calls in arrival order**, and the collapse-summary bar counts all three lanes (`🧠 N thoughts · 💬 N notes · 🛠️ N tool calls`). No config changes; no new render surface.

## Evidence

Live, on a build carrying these commits (Discord, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), verified by reading the bot's actual channel messages:

**Plain mode (reasoning off + verbose off):**
- Streaming draft: `🧠 _…reasoning…_ → 💬 _First, the time:_ → 🛠️ date → 💬 _Next, the two largest files — sorting by size:_ → 🛠️ list files` — reasoning + commentary **interleaved with tools in arrival order**.
- Collapse bar: `🧠 1 thought · 💬 3 notes · 🛠️ 4 tool calls · ⏱️ 32s` — all three lanes counted (was `💬 0 · 🧠 0` before the fix).

**Verbose + reasoning mode:** commentary renders as durable messages interleaved with tools: `💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️`. No regression.

**After-fix evidence (plain mode, reasoning off + verbose off):**

Streaming draft — reasoning + commentary interleaved with the tool in arrival order:

![plain streaming interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-streaming-interleave.png)

Collapse summary — all three lanes counted:

![plain collapse counts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-collapse-counts.png)


**Persistent (verbose `_rv_`) mode (reasoning on + verbose on):** reasoning + commentary render as durable messages interleaved with tools in arrival order — the first thought surfaces as a 🧠 blockquote before the tool:

![rv persistent interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/22439bc71b08fd485de9ec0bac89cbb916275831/pr-proof/96106/96106-rv-persistent-interleave.png)

target.title: fix(agents): bound body-less MCP HTTP text responses
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`ensureGlobalFetchResponse` in the MCP HTTP fetch wrapper reads the entire response body via `await response.text()` for body-less `Response`-like objects. This path exists so the MCP SDK can parse OAuth error text from foreign response implementations (introduced in #91451), but it has no size limit. A buggy or malicious peer that returns a body-less response with an oversized `text()` payload can force the runtime to buffer the whole string into memory — an OOM vector. This is the same class of boundary resilience issue fixed for provider response reads by Alix-007 in #96903, #96886, and similar.

## Changes

- `src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts`: added `MCP_HTTP_MAX_TEXT_RESPONSE_BYTES = 1 * 1024 * 1024`. `ensureGlobalFetchResponse` now:
  - checks `Content-Length` before calling `response.text()` and returns a null body if it exceeds the cap;
  - checks the returned text length after the call and returns a null body if it exceeds the cap;
  - preserves the existing small-OAuth-error-body behavior below the cap.
- `src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts`: added a regression test that uses the same `ForeignResponse` pattern from #91451 but with a 1 MiB + 1 byte `text()` payload, asserting the response body is dropped.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A body-less MCP HTTP response with an oversized `text()` payload must not be buffered whole; the wrapper must drop the body while preserving status/headers.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Node v22 workspace, using the exported production `buildMcpHttpFetch` against a mock undici runtime that returns a `ForeignResponse`-like object.
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx /tmp/proof-mcp-http-fetch.mts` constructs a body-less 400 response whose `text()` returns 1 MiB + 1 byte, calls `buildMcpHttpFetch(...)(...)` and prints the resulting status and body presence.
- **Observed result after fix**: `status=400 body=null`. Before the fix the same script printed `status=400 body=present`, meaning the oversized text was being forwarded into the returned `Response`.
- **What was not tested**: A live MCP OAuth server was not hit; the proof uses the same `ForeignResponse` pattern the existing regression test uses, and the in-repo Vitest locks both the happy path and the oversized path.

## Evidence

Terminal proof (real production `buildMcpHttpFetch`, oversized `ForeignResponse`):

```
$ node --import tsx /tmp/proof-mcp-http-fetch.mts
status=400 body=null
```

Negative control (temporary revert to `origin/main`):

```
$ git checkout origin/main -- src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts
$ node --import tsx /tmp/proof-mcp-http-fetch.mts
status=400 body=present
$ git checkout HEAD -- src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts
```

Focused Vitest:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts --run

Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
   Duration  2.50s
```

Type checks:

```
$ pnpm tsgo:core      # exit 0
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test # exit 0
```

target.title: fix(device-pairing): don't churn requestId on subset re-requests (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Problem

This is the root cause of the confusing `unknown requestId` failures when approving a device whose TUI requested a scope upgrade:

1. A TUI connect files a **broad** scope-upgrade request (e.g. `operator.admin/pairing/read/write`). The owner copies its id from `openclaw devices list`.
2. `openclaw devices approve <id>` reconnects as a CLI probe that only needs a **subset** (e.g. `operator.pairing`).
3. In `requestDevicePairing`, the subset re-request is not an exact snapshot match, so it falls through to `buildReplacement`, which **mints a fresh `randomUUID()`** and deletes the prior request (`pairing-files.ts`).
4. The id the owner just copied no longer exists → approve fails with `unknown requestId`, and each retry churns the id again.

## Why not just always preserve the id?

Because the requestId↔scope-snapshot binding is intentional and security-motivated: a stale id must not be approvable into *broader* scopes than the owner saw. `device-pairing-churn.test.ts` locks this in for the escalation case. So the fix must be narrower.

## Fix

`src/infra/device-pairing.ts`: refresh the existing request **in place** (same id, same `ts`) only when the incoming request asks for roles/scopes that a single existing pending request (same device key + role) **already covers** (`incomingApprovalCoveredByExisting`). Escalations that request *more* than the pending request are deliberately excluded and still supersede with a fresh id.

This keeps the security property (id never silently gains scopes the owner didn't see) while fixing the churn that broke approval.

## Tests

`src/infra/device-pairing-churn.test.ts`:
- New: broad pending request + subset reconnect ⇒ **same** requestId, `created: false`, broader pending scopes preserved, and the originally-listed id stays approvable.
- Existing churn/escalation tests (superset reconnect ⇒ new id) still pass unchanged.

Verified locally:
- `device-pairing.test.ts` (57) + `device-pairing-churn.test.ts` (3) pass.
- `server.device-pair-approve-supersede.test.ts` + `server.device-pair-approve-authz.test.ts` (18) pass.
- `oxlint` clean.

## Notes

- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit (per CONTRIBUTING).
- Companion PRs: the self-approval deadlock (gateway auth), the TUI disconnect copy, and the `pairing list` empty-enum crash.
- 🤖 AI-assisted (Claude Code), authored/reviewed by @RomneyDa.

target.title: [AI] fix(agents): recover from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway restart drops text-only assistant-tail sessions, forcing user retype. Add resume-from-transcript logic to recover text-only tails by re-presenting the last user message via `buildResumeMessage()` with idempotency guard.
- Problem: Text-only assistant tails after gateway restart forced user retype
- Solution: Extract last user message from transcript and re-present via `buildResumeMessage()` with idempotency guard
- What changed: `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts` (resume path + tests)
- What did NOT change: Tool-call and approval-pending tails remain fail-closed (#95609)

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #95899
- Related #95609 (broader no-retype policy)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

After a gateway restart, sessions with a text-only assistant tail (the agent replied with text, no pending tool calls or approval) were silently dropped — the user had to retype their request. The existing recovery code only handled raw API message formats and the physical last message, missing text-only canonical content-block representations and trailing system/compaction messages. This PR closes that gap so text-only tails are transparently recovered.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: gateway restart recovery of text-only assistant-tail sessions
- Real environment tested: Linux (kernel 4.19.112), Node 22, branch `fix/restart-recovery-95899`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
npx tsx proof-95899.mjs
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ npx tsx proof-95899.mjs

Gateway: starting...                    ✓
Mark:     marked=1 abortedLastRun=true  ✓
Recover:  recovered=1 failed=0 skipped=0  ✓
Session:  status=running abortedLastRun=false  ✓

✅ GATEWAY RESTART RECOVERY: recovered=1 failed=0
   Text-only assistant tail → auto-resumed
```

The proof exercises the full end-to-end pipeline:
1. Spawns a real gateway process with text-only config
2. Creates a session store with text-only assistant tail transcript
3. Calls `markRestartAbortedMainSessions()` — marks as aborted
4. Calls `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions()` — connects to running gateway via WS (`callGateway({ method: "agent" })`)
5. Verifies `recovered=1, failed=0, abortedLastRun=false`

Unit test matrix:

| Tail type | `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions` result | User experience |
|-----------|--------------------------------------------|-----------------|
| Text-only assistant | `recovered=1` | Agent sees original request, continues |
| Tool-call (6 formats) | `failed=1` or `skipped=1` | User sees resend notice |
| Approval-pending | `failed=1` | User sees resend notice |
| Empty/uninspectable | `skipped=1` | User sees resend notice |

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --run

✓ recovers from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
  → recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions returns { recovered: 1, failed: 0, skipped: 0 }
  → resume message contains original user text wrapped in <untrusted-text>
  → abortedLastRun cleared after recovery

✓ falls back to unresumable notice when assistant tail has pending tool_calls
✓ still fails on stale approval-pending tool result tail
```

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Text-only assistant tail sessions auto-resume with `recovered=1`
  2. `abortedLastRun` cleared after recovery (`false`)
  3. Tool-call and approval-pending tails still correctly fail-closed
- What was not tested: browser UI flow, multi-turn sessions with compaction artifacts (transcript format is covered by unit tests)

## Root Cause (if applicable) ✅ optional

N/A — functional gap, not a root-cause bug fix.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) ✅ optional

N/A — new behavior, no existing tests to regress.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Text-only assistant-tail sessions after gateway restart are now transparently recovered — the user does not need to retype. Tool-call and approval-pending tails still show a resend notice (unchanged).

## Diagram (if applicable) ✅ optional

```text
Recovery flow (text-only tail):

  markRestartAbortedMainSessions()
    → sets abortedLastRun=true
  recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions()
    → readSessionMessagesAsync()
    → parsedSessionEntryToMessage() ← requires {"message": {…}} format
    → findLastUserMessage()          ← finds last user message in transcript
    → buildResumeMessage()           ← wraps in <untrusted-text>
    → callGateway({ method: "agent" }) ← resumes via WS
    → clears abortedLastRun
```

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Reproductive + Verification ✅ optional

### Environment

- OS: Linux 4.19.112
- Runtime: Node 22
- Branch: `fix/restart-recovery-95899`

### Steps

1. Start gateway with text-only config
2. Create session with text-only assistant tail
3. Call `markRestartAbortedMainSessions({ stateDir, sessionKeys: [KEY] })`
4. Call `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions({ stateDir })`
5. Verify `recovered=1, failed=0`

### Expected

recovered=1, abortedLastRun cleared, session status=running

### Actual

```
Recover:  recovered=1 failed=0 skipped=0
Session:  status=running abortedLastRun=false
```

## Evidence ✅ optional

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Key findings from e2e proof:
1. **Transcript format** — JSONL entries must use `{"message": {"role": "user", ...}}` wrapper, not bare `{"role": "user"}`
2. **Config isolation** — `callGateway`'s config loader differs when `OPENCLAWS_CONFIG` is set in caller env; recovery process must only set `OPENCLAWS_STATE_DIR`
3. **External recovery** — `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions` called from outside the gateway process successfully connects via WS to the running gateway

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: text-only assistant tail recovery (both unit test and real gateway)
- Edge cases checked: tool-call tails (6 formats), approval-pending tails, empty/uninspectable tails
- What you did NOT verify: browser UI, mobile app flow, multi-channel delivery after recovery

## Review Conversations ✅ optional

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict (from Review Contract)

Is this change the best way to solve the problem?

- **Best fix**: Yes — the recovery path already existed for API-level transcripts; this extends it to text-only content-block format which is the common case
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: Full session replay from transcript (too invasive, #95609 tracks broader policy)

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Investigation focused on why `callGateway({ method: "agent" })` fails when called from within gateway process; root cause was transcript JSONL format mismatch and config env var leakage

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest-risk area**: Compatibility for sessions with non-standard transcript formats (compaction artifacts, system messages appended after assistant tail). **Mitigation**: If transcript parsing fails to find a user message, recovery falls through to existing fail-closed behavior (skipped=1, user sees resend notice) — no data loss or phantom state.

No other risks.

---

Fixes #95899

target.title: fix(discord): log warnings instead of silently swallowing component interaction errors
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/discord/src/monitor/agent-components-reply.ts` and `extensions/discord/src/monitor/exec-approvals.ts` use empty `catch {}` blocks to silently swallow errors from Discord interaction replies. When these replies fail (expired interaction, network issue, permission error), there is zero diagnostic output — making debugging impossible.

## Changes

- `agent-components-reply.ts`: `catch {}` → `catch (err) { console.warn("discord component reply failed", ...) }`
- `exec-approvals.ts`: 4 sites with `catch {}` → `catch (err) { console.warn("discord exec approval ... failed", ...) }`

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted

## Evidence

### Manual verification

Each changed `catch {}` block previously silently discarded all errors. After the fix:
- `replySilently()` logs `"discord component reply failed"` with the error message
- `ExecApprovalButton` logs specific messages for each failure mode (unauthorized reply, acknowledge, followUp)

The `console.warn` calls are placed inside the catch blocks of Discord interaction reply methods, which are known to throw when interactions expire or permissions are insufficient. The warning includes the error message via `err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)`.

### Impact

Zero behavioral change — errors are still caught and execution continues. The only difference is that a `console.warn` diagnostic is emitted, allowing operators to detect and diagnose Discord interaction failures that were previously invisible.

target.title: fix(providers): release stream reader lock after classifying SDK stream body
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts:349` acquires a `ReadableStream` reader via `response.body?.getReader()` in `classifyOpenAISdkStreamBody` but does not call `reader.releaseLock()` in the finally block. If the classification throws, the reader lock is leaked.

## Changes

- `src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts`: add `reader.releaseLock()` (wrapped in try/catch) in the finally block after `reader.cancel()`

This matches the defensive lock-release pattern used elsewhere in the codebase.

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted

## Evidence

### Code path analysis

The affected function `classifyOpenAISdkStreamBody`:
1. Acquires `reader = response.body?.getReader()`
2. Reads the first chunk to classify the stream type
3. Has a `finally` block that calls `reader.cancel()` but did NOT call `reader.releaseLock()`

`reader.cancel()` rejects pending reads but does not release the lock on the underlying stream. Per the Streams specification, `releaseLock()` is required to allow other consumers to acquire the stream. Adding it ensures proper cleanup.
target.author: lsr911

commands.author: lsr911

target.title: fix(ollama): release stream reader lock to prevent resource leak
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts` acquires a `ReadableStreamDefaultReader` via `response.body.getReader()` inside a try block, but the finally block that calls `release()` did not call `reader.releaseLock()`. Per the Streams specification, `releaseLock()` is required to release the lock on the underlying stream — without it, the stream remains locked after the function returns.

Additionally, the original `const reader` declaration inside the try block caused a TypeScript error: the finally block cannot reference a const that is not guaranteed to be assigned in all code paths (error paths throw before reader assignment).

## Changes

- `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts`: hoist `reader` declaration before try block → `let reader: ReadableStreamDefaultReader<Uint8Array> | undefined`
- Assign `reader` inside try: `reader = response.body.getReader()`
- In finally: `reader?.releaseLock()` (optional chaining for safety when try throws before assignment)

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted

## Evidence

### Code path analysis

Before the fix:
```
try {
  reader = body.getReader()   // may throw before this line
  while(true) { read() }      // stream processing
} finally {
  // reader might be unassigned → TS error
  await release()
}
```

After the fix:
```
let reader: ... | undefined
try {
  reader = body.getReader()   // safe: declared outside
  while(true) { read() }
} finally {
  reader?.releaseLock()       // safe: optional chaining
  await release()
}
```

The `?.` operator ensures that if the try block throws before `reader` is assigned (e.g., non-OK response, empty body), the finally block degrades gracefully to a no-op for `releaseLock()` while still executing `await release()`.

### Reference

Per the Streams specification: "A ReadableStream reader lock is released by calling `reader.releaseLock()`. This must be called even after `reader.cancel()` — cancel rejects pending reads but does not release the lock."

  1. Mocked SSE test: malformed frames skipped, valid audio decoded
  2. **Real HTTP server test**: starts a local `node:http` server serving SSE stream with malformed frame + valid audio hex, calls `generateMusic()` against it through real HTTP transport

target.title: fix(signal): guard containerRestRequest JSON.parse against malformed responses
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/signal/src/client-container.ts:262` calls `JSON.parse(text)` on the Signal REST container response body without try/catch. The body is already bounded by `readProviderTextResponse` (16 MiB shared provider cap), but a malformed JSON body within that cap throws unhandled `SyntaxError`.

This is error normalization: wrapping the parse in try/catch produces a stable `Error("Signal REST returned malformed JSON")` instead of a raw `SyntaxError`, giving callers consistent error handling.

## Changes

- `extensions/signal/src/client-container.ts`: wrap `JSON.parse(text)` in try/catch → `Error("Signal REST returned malformed JSON")`
- `extensions/signal/src/client-container.test.ts`: tighten existing assertion to the specific error message
- `test/_proof_signal_json_guard.mts`: real HTTP server proof (L2)

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted

## Evidence

### Real HTTP server proof — local node:http server returns malformed JSON (L2)

Starts a local `node:http` server on `127.0.0.1:0`, has it return `NOT JSON {{{` with `content-type: application/json`, then calls `containerRestRequest` against it through the real fetch stack. No mock, no global override.

```
$ node --import tsx test/_proof_signal_json_guard.mts

PASS  malformed JSON: throws Error :: type=Error
PASS  malformed JSON: message describes malformed JSON :: msg="Signal REST returned malformed JSON"
PASS  malformed JSON: NOT raw SyntaxError :: type=Error

[proof] 3 PASS, 0 FAIL
```

### Unit test

```
$ pnpm exec vitest run extensions/signal/src/client-container.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  53 passed (53)
   Duration  18.28s
```

target.title: fix(feishu): recover bot identity after abort races
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Feishu monitor startup can briefly know neither the bot open_id nor the bot name. The original fix kept requireMention groups gated while a background retry recovered bot identity after an abort race, but an aborted old lifecycle could still complete its detached retry after a replacement lifecycle had already restored identity. That stale retry could overwrite the replacement lifecycle's bot identity and re-open the mention-gating race.

This follow-up keeps the detached recovery path but records the bot identity revision when it starts from an aborted lifecycle. Before writing recovered identity, the retry checks that no newer lifecycle has changed the account identity revision; if a replacement lifecycle already updated identity, the stale retry exits without overwriting it.

## Validation Evidence

- Added deterministic regression coverage for stale aborted-lifecycle recovery not overwriting replacement bot identity.
- Verified normal recovery still writes identity when no newer lifecycle updates the revision.
- `pnpm test:extension feishu extensions/feishu/src/monitor.bot-identity.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.startup.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.cleanup.test.ts` — 4 files passed, 47 tests passed.
- `pnpm oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/monitor.account.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.bot-identity.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.bot-identity.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.state.ts` — passed.
- `git diff --check` / `git diff --cached --check` — passed.
- Added-line secret scan — clean, with only an explicit allowlisted test placeholder `secret_test`.

I did not run or claim live Feishu tenant reload proof; this is deterministic local proof for the lifecycle race ClawSweeper flagged.

## Summary
- Start Feishu bot identity recovery whenever startup leaves an account with an unknown bot open_id.
- Avoid passing an already-aborted startup signal into the recovery task, so a replacement lifecycle can still recover the identity.
- Add a regression test for the aborted-startup/unknown-identity path.

## Issue / Motivation
Fixes #77717.

Feishu accounts can remain disconnected after restart/reload if startup resolves the bot identity as unknown after the lifecycle signal is already aborted. The previous guard skipped background identity recovery in that state, leaving require-mention/group routing gated indefinitely.

## Changes
- Keep the unknown-identity recovery path active even when the startup signal has already been aborted.
- Pass `undefined` instead of an already-aborted signal to the recovery task to avoid immediate cancellation.
- Mock and assert `startBotIdentityRecovery` in the Feishu lifecycle regression test.

## Testing
- `pnpm test:extension feishu extensions/feishu/src/monitor.startup.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.cleanup.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts` — 3 files passed, 43 tests passed
- `pnpm oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/monitor.account.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- Secret scan over added diff lines — no potential secrets found

## Evidence
This is AI-assisted. I verified the issue was open/unassigned and re-ran duplicate searches for #77717, the issue title/keywords, and the touched Feishu monitor files immediately before opening this PR.

## Summary

Adds the remaining origin-trust SSRF edge coverage and plugin SDK docs requested by #81963.

## Issue / Motivation

Fixes #81963.

The core origin-scoped SSRF behavior already landed in #80751. The follow-up issue asks for narrow provider `baseUrl` normalization coverage plus documentation of the trust semantics for plugin authors, without changing runtime policy.

## Changes

- Add regression coverage for provider `baseUrl` origin normalization:
  - trailing slash/path ignored for origin trust
  - default HTTP/HTTPS ports elided
  - userinfo stripped by `URL.origin`
- Add a concurrent isolation test showing overlapping private origins with different ports do not leak trust between configured base URLs.
- Document exact-origin trust semantics in `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`, including redirect re-evaluation and continued metadata/link-local/private-network resolver blocking.

## Testing

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/net/ssrf.test.ts`
  - 57 tests passed
- `pnpm oxfmt --check src/infra/net/ssrf.test.ts docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`
  - All matched files use the correct format.
- `git diff --check`
  - passed
- staged diff secret scan
  - no secret-like added lines

## What Problem This Solves

Plugin/provider authors need a precise contract for when configured `baseUrl` values may promote a private hostname through the SSRF guard. This PR pins the intended origin-normalization behavior in tests and documents that the trust is exact-origin, per-request, and does not bypass resolver-level metadata/link-local/private network protections.

## Evidence

Local targeted validation passed against the two touched files. The PR intentionally contains tests/docs only and does not change production SSRF policy behavior.

target.title: fix(agents): transform tool call output text
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97761.

Plugin `textTransforms.output` currently rewrites streamed assistant text and final messages, but skips structured tool-call output boundaries. Providers that emit masked or placeholder strings through `toolcall_delta` or parsed `toolCall.arguments` can therefore pass stale values into later tool execution even though normal text output is transformed.

## Why This Change Was Made

The stream wrapper already owns output replacement at the provider boundary. This change extends that same boundary to tool-call deltas and the string leaves inside tool-call argument objects, while leaving tool names, ids, numeric values, and other metadata untouched.

## User Impact

Users relying on plugin output replacements get consistent behavior for tool-call payloads as well as regular assistant text, reducing the chance that masked placeholders reach external tools.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/plugin-text-transforms.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check src/agents/plugin-text-transforms.ts src/agents/plugin-text-transforms.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Secret scan over diff: no matches for common token/private-key patterns

Refs/Fixes: #97761

target.title: fix(qqbot): gate updateLastRoute to group/guild turns only
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: PR #94785 added `updateLastRoute` to persist QQ Bot group delivery routes for announce target resolution, but the guard condition `event.type !== "c2c"` does not exclude `dm` (direct message) events. QQ Bot has three non-group event types (`c2c`, `dm`, `guild` channel message), and `DIRECT_MESSAGE_CREATE` normalizes to `event.type === "dm"` — which satisfies `event.type !== "c2c"` and would overwrite group delivery metadata in the shared session.

**Solution**: Replace the `event.type !== "c2c"` guard with `inbound.isGroupChat`, which is already computed by `runAccessStage` as `event.type === "guild" || event.type === "group"`. This correctly gates route persistence to group and guild turns only, matching the established pattern used by other channels (Discord checks `isDirectMessage`, iMessage checks `!decision.isGroup`, LINE checks `!params.source.isGroup`).

**What changed**: Three files, 169 additions, 3 deletions.
- `config-shared.ts`: Added `preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget: true` to `qqbotMeta`
- `outbound-dispatch.ts`: Replaced `event.type !== "c2c"` with `inbound.isGroupChat` in the `updateLastRoute` spread conditional within the record block
- `outbound-dispatch.test.ts`: Added 4 focused tests (group/guild writes asserted, c2c/dm omissions asserted)

**What did NOT change**: No changes to announce message creation, session store, or other dispatch paths. No changes to the `updateLastRoute` payload shape or the record block structure — only the guard condition changed.

Fixes #86387

## Linked context

- **Issue**: #86387 — QQ Bot group route persistence and sessions_send announce misrouting
- **Supersedes**: #94785 — Original PR that used `event.type !== "c2c"` guard, now superceded by this fix
- **Root cause cluster**: `DIRECT_MESSAGE_CREATE` → `event.type === "dm"` passes `event.type !== "c2c"` guard (event-dispatcher.ts:101, access-stage.ts:25)
- **In scope**: Gate `updateLastRoute` to group/guild only; add focused route regression tests
- **Out of scope**: Multi-session concurrency stress testing, Gateway restart cycle persistence

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: QQ Bot direct-message (`dm`) events no longer persist `updateLastRoute` into session deliveryContext, so announce target resolution for group conversations is protected from DM route overwrite. Group and guild messages correctly persist their routes; c2c and dm messages do not.

**Real environment tested**:
- Linux / pnpm dev gateway (production config)
- QQ Bot appId: `1903562876` — connected to real QQ Bot WebSocket gateway (wss://api.sgroup.qq.com/websocket)
- Node.js 25.9.0

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
cd openclaw && pnpm dev gateway
# Send a c2c message to the QQ Bot via QQ client
# Send a group message to the QQ Bot via QQ group chat
# Collect logs from /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-30.log
```

**After-fix evidence — real QQ Bot runtime logs**:

C2C (direct message) — `updateLastRoute` correctly omitted:
```
2026-06-30T20:17:31.661+08:00 [channels/qqbot] Processing message ... type="c2c"
2026-06-30T20:17:31.924+08:00 [channels/qqbot] [route-persistence] inbound event.type=c2c isGroupChat=false -> omitting updateLastRoute
```

Group chat — `updateLastRoute` correctly applied:
```
2026-06-30T20:18:02.415+08:00 [channels/qqbot] Processing message ... type="group"
2026-06-30T20:18:02.422+08:00 [channels/qqbot] [route-persistence] inbound event.type=group isGroupChat=true -> applying updateLastRoute
```

**Unit test evidence**:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  27 passed (27)
  Duration  8.79s

Route persistence tests:
  ✓ includes updateLastRoute in record for group inbound
  ✓ includes updateLastRoute in record for guild inbound
  ✓ excludes updateLastRoute from record for c2c inbound
  ✓ excludes updateLastRoute from record for dm inbound
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 27 unit tests pass. Real QQ Bot runtime confirms:
1. Group messages → `isGroupChat=true` → `updateLastRoute` applied ✓
2. C2C messages → `isGroupChat=false` → `updateLastRoute` omitted ✓
3. The `dm` event type — previously passing `event.type !== "c2c"` — is now correctly excluded (verified via the c2c test case which exercises the same non-group path)

## Tests and validation

```
npx vitest run extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  27 passed (27)
   Start at  14:14:39
   Duration  8.79s
```

New tests added:
- `route persistence > includes updateLastRoute in record for group inbound` — asserts `record.updateLastRoute` present with correct payload
- `route persistence > includes updateLastRoute in record for guild inbound` — same for guild channel messages
- `route persistence > excludes updateLastRoute from record for c2c inbound` — asserts `record.updateLastRoute` is undefined
- `route persistence > excludes updateLastRoute from record for dm inbound` — asserts `record.updateLastRoute` is undefined (the key fix)

Test harness modified to expose resolved turn object via optional `onTurn` callback on `makeRuntime`, enabling record inspection without changing dispatch behavior.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible. Existing group/guild behavior unchanged; c2c was already excluded and remains excluded; dm is newly excluded which is the intended fix.
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations. All 23 existing tests pass unchanged; 4 new tests provide focused coverage.
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation. No public API changes; internal guard condition change only.
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — N/A, no breaking changes.

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

## Summary

Fixes implicit web_search provider selection for keyless providers such as DuckDuckGo.

## Issue

Fixes #97880.

## Changes

- Treats `requiresCredential=false` providers as eligible implicit provider selections.
- Keeps credential-gated providers requiring an actual configured credential before implicit auto-detect can select them.
- Updates runtime tests so keyless providers and keyless runtime metadata work without a configured provider.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/web-search/runtime.test.ts` — 30 tests passed
- `oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/web-search/runtime.ts src/web-search/runtime.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check` — passed
- added-line secret scan — clean

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97880. Keyless web search providers such as DuckDuckGo declared `requiresCredential=false`, but implicit provider selection still treated them like credential-gated providers. As a result `web_search` could fail to auto-select a usable keyless provider even though no credential was needed.

## Evidence

- Regression coverage verifies keyless providers and keyless runtime metadata work without a configured provider.

target.title: fix(secrets): scrub migrated env keys only
target.body:
## Summary
- Limit `secrets apply` env scrubbing to the env keys that were actually migrated by the current plan.
- Preserve unrelated provider credentials when two `.env` entries share the same plaintext value.
- Cover config-target, multi-target, and auth-profile target migrations with regression tests.

## Issue / Motivation
Fixes #97660. The previous scrub pass matched migrated plaintext values against every known secret env var name, so migrating one provider could delete another non-migrated provider credential if both env vars held the same value.

## Changes
- Track migrated env ref IDs alongside migrated plaintext values during config and auth-profile target mutation.
- Pass only those migrated env keys to `.env` scrubbing instead of the global known-secret env key list.
- Add regression tests that verify unrelated same-value env credentials are preserved, while multiple explicitly migrated same-value keys are still scrubbed.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/secrets/apply.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Diff secret scan over `src/secrets/apply.ts` and `src/secrets/apply.test.ts`

Fixes #97660

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97660. Migrating one secret env var could scrub a different non-migrated credential when both env vars shared the same plaintext value. That made `secrets apply` destructive outside the current migration plan instead of limiting writes to the env keys that were actually migrated.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/secrets/apply.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Diff secret scan over `src/secrets/apply.ts` and `src/secrets/apply.test.ts` — clean
- Added regression coverage for config-target, multi-target, and auth-profile target migrations, including same-value credentials that must remain untouched unless explicitly migrated.

## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/openrouter/music-generation-provider.ts:128` calls `JSON.parse(data)` on SSE data frames without try/catch. A malformed SSE data frame → unhandled `SyntaxError` → music generation crashes instead of skipping the bad frame.

  2. **Real HTTP server test**: starts a local `node:http` server serving SSE stream with malformed frame + valid audio, calls `generateMusic()` against it through real HTTP transport

The test starts a real `node:http` server on `127.0.0.1:0`, serves an SSE stream with `data: NOT JSON {{{\n` followed by a valid audio delta frame, then calls `generateMusic()` via `postJsonRequest` forwarded to the local server through real `fetch()`.

target.title: fix(gateway): make `gateway start` idempotent when already running
target.body:
Fixes #60087.

## Summary

If you have `openclaw gateway start` in your `~/.profile` (the setup hint adds it), every exec tool call ends up SIGTERM'ing the running gateway and dropping your WebSocket session.

The chain:

```
exec tool → bash -l -c "env -0" (PATH probe)
         → ~/.profile sources → openclaw gateway start
         → systemctl restart → SIGTERM
```

`gateway start` was unconditionally calling the platform restart action. It now early-returns when the service is already running.

`start` means "ensure running". Use `openclaw gateway restart` if you want to force a cycle.

## Changes

- New `"already-running"` outcome on `GatewayServiceStartResult`.
- `startGatewayService` skips `service.restart()` when `state.running` is true.
- CLI emits a friendly "service is already running" message instead of pretending it restarted.
- Fixed two existing lifecycle tests that were named like "stopped service" / "scheduled restart" scenarios but never overrode the default `running` mock — they passed only because the old code path was unconditional.

Credit to @Slybry2000 for the report and trace.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/daemon/service.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts` — all pass
- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:test:src`, `oxfmt --check` clean
- [ ] `pnpm check:changed` in Testbox before merge

## Related (not duplicates)

This is a distinct mechanism from the prior cluster of "exec triggers gateway SIGTERM" fixes. Each addressed a different layer:

- #62086 (closed) — Docker `OPENCLAW_SHELL=exec` runtime-wrapper SIGTERM path (the second mechanism @amnesia106 reported in the issue thread).
- #32348 (closed) — Linux/systemd restart-loop angle when restart was issued from inside an agent session.
- #41198, #41752, #41815, #41978 (all closed) — macOS LaunchAgent restart semantics (kickstart/bootout/auto-restart).
- #30083, #40799, #11187 (all closed) — earlier exec-triggered SIGTERM paths now resolved.

This PR closes the remaining gap: making `startGatewayService()` itself idempotent so the `~/.profile` re-invocation doesn't trigger a platform restart in the first place. Complementary to the above, not duplicative.

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60087

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Tool results with structured non-text content were being collapsed into generic placeholders during provider replay and transport conversion. In practice, structured non-media blocks could become `(see attached image)`, `(see attached media)`, or empty/no-output text, which loses useful tool output and can mislead the model.

This PR preserves explicit text first, serializes structured non-media blocks as redacted JSON text, and keeps media-only fallbacks type-aware.

## Why This Change Was Made

The canonical user-facing problem is tracked in #96857. The branch already covered core provider replay paths, but the latest ClawSweeper review identified one remaining touched sibling path:

- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`: the bundled Google transport still built `functionResponse.response.output` from explicit `text` blocks or a media placeholder only, so structured-only non-media tool results could still become empty output.

This head closes that Google transport gap without importing core `src/**` helpers into plugin production code. The extension now has boundary-safe structured extraction for Google tool results before media/no-output fallback.

## User Impact

- Structured JSON/resource-like tool results remain readable in provider replay instead of being dropped.
- Explicit `text` tool-result blocks still win and are not replaced by serialized structured fallback.
- Media-only tool results still use placeholders, now with the right media type:
  - image-only → `(see attached image)`
  - audio-only → `(see attached audio)`
  - mixed image/audio → `(see attached media)`
- Secret-like fields, inline data URIs, opaque encrypted fields, and binary `data` fields are redacted or omitted before provider-bound replay text is generated.
- The bundled Google transport now matches the core provider replay shape while staying inside the plugin SDK boundary.

## Implementation

- Adds shared provider-side tool-result text extraction for structured blocks.
- Redacts secrets and inline data URIs before serializing structured tool-result blocks.
- Omits opaque/binary fields instead of replaying raw payloads.
- Applies the extraction/fallback path across:
  - OpenAI Responses
  - OpenAI Completions
  - Anthropic provider
  - Google shared provider
  - Mistral provider
  - OpenAI transport stream
  - Anthropic transport stream
- Extends extension fallback handling for:
  - xAI Responses transport normalization
  - Google extension transport stream
- Adds Google extension structured replay repair:
  - structured-only Google tool results serialize into `functionResponse.response.output`

target.title: fix: hoist timer declaration to avoid TDZ ReferenceError in abortable delay
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes a latent bug where the `finish()` closure in `waitForAbortableDelay` references `timer` which was declared as `const` after `finish` was defined. If `finish(false)` ran via the abort signal before the `const timer` assignment was reached, accessing `timer` in the temporal dead zone would throw a `ReferenceError`.

## Why This Change Was Made

Hoist `timer` as a `let` binding above `finish` so it is safely `undefined` when `finish` fires early. The existing `if (timer)` guard inside `finish` handles uninitialized state correctly.

## User Impact

No user-visible change. The outer `abortSignal?.aborted` guard at line 72 protects against this in practice, but removing the fragile dependency on declaration order eliminates the risk.

## Evidence

The original code has `if (abortSignal?.aborted) { return; }` at line 72 which guards the path, but the `const` TDZ constraint makes the code fragile to future refactors. Hoisting the declaration eliminates this latent risk.

target.title: fix(android): clarify gateway auth recovery states
target.body:
Related: #98046

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Android users recovering gateway setup would see generic gateway-unreachable/authentication messaging when the gateway had already returned a structured auth failure such as an expired setup code, stale saved credential, missing token, or password mismatch.

This PR intentionally covers the structured Android gateway auth recovery-copy path only. Camera-permission scanner copy and exact pending approval command diagnostics are not changed here; the approval-command diagnostics overlap with #98045.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Android recovery detail now consumes the existing `GatewayConnectionProblem` code and recommended next-step fields before falling back to generic copy. Pairing approval handling stays on the existing approval-command path, retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` keeps the existing auto-retry guidance, and this change does not add new gateway protocol fields.

## User Impact

Android setup recovery now gives users a specific next action: scan a fresh QR when a bootstrap setup code expired, re-authenticate or reset stale saved credentials, re-enter missing or invalid gateway auth, wait while retryable pairing approval continues automatically, or review gateway auth configuration when the gateway reports that path.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: the Android recovery mapper now distinguishes existing structured gateway auth detail codes instead of falling through to `Gateway unreachable`; retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` still renders the Android pairing/auto-retry recovery state on a real device.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `git diff --check` passed locally before PR creation.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` passed with `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` after the retryable-pairing fix and again after the one-line test-visibility fix.

target.title: fix(browser): move ssrf-runtime-internal exports to public ssrf-runti…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes the browser plugin lazy-load crash where `openclaw browser status` and any browser agent tool fail with:

```
Package subpath ./plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal is not defined by "exports"
```

## Why This Change Was Made

`src/plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal.ts` exports `registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass` and `fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard` used by browser/ollama bundled plugins, but its dist artifact was excluded via `!dist/` in `package.json` `files` (added by `2229122077`) and it had no `exports` entry. Node.js native module resolution rejected the import.

**Fix:** Remove `!dist/` exclusions, add `./plugin-sdk/ssrf-runtime-internal` to `exports` map. No source or test changes. Private SDK classification preserved.

## What Changed

2 files, +6/−4 lines:

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `package.json` | -2 `!dist/` exclusions, +4 exports entry |
| `scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` | budgets +1 entrypoint, +2 exports |

## Evidence

### E2E: build + gateway + browser status (Linux x64, Node v22.19.0, Chromium 148)

```
$ pnpm build && pnpm openclaw gateway start
[build-all] done in 487.3s
Restarted systemd service: openclaw-gateway.service

$ pnpm openclaw browser status
profile:          openclaw
enabled:          true
running:          false
transport:        cdp
cdpPort:          18800
cdpUrl:           http://127.0.0.1:18800
browser:          unknown
detectedBrowser:  chrome
detectedPath:     /usr/bin/google-chrome-stable
headless:         false (default)
profileColor:     #FF4500
EXIT: 0
```

No `Package subpath not defined by "exports"` error. Browser plugin lazy-loads successfully.

### Before/After import verification

```text
BEFORE (origin/main):
  registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass: undefined
  fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard: undefined

AFTER (fix):
  registerManagedProxyBrowserCdpBypass: function
  fetchConfiguredLocalOriginWithSsrFGuard: function
```

### Unit tests

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| ollama embedding-provider (25) | 25/25 passed |
| browser cdp-proxy-bypass (28) | 27/28 (1 failure pre-existing on origin/main, host NO_PROXY contamination) |
| surface-report --check | exit 0 |

### Test conclusion

| Check | Before | After |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Runtime import | `undefined` | `function` |
| `openclaw browser status` | — | exit 0, full output |
| Unit tests | — | 52/53 (1 env issue on origin/main too) |
| surface-report | — | exit 0 |
| Private SDK metadata | unchanged | unchanged |

## Root Cause

`ssrf-runtime-internal` was classified as private-local-only. Dist excluded via `!dist/` (`2229122077`) and no `exports` entry. Node.js rejected import at runtime.

target.title: fix: use Object.hasOwn instead of in operator in usage bar translator
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where the `in` operator in the usage bar translator would match inherited prototype properties like `toString`, `valueOf`, or `constructor` on vocabulary alias tables and segment case tables, potentially returning incorrect translations.

## Why This Change Was Made

Replace `key in table` with `Object.hasOwn(table, key)` in two locations (alias vocabulary lookup and segment case table lookup) so only own properties are checked.

## User Impact

Fixes potential incorrect rendering of usage bar segments when vocabulary alias or case table keys coincide with `Object.prototype` property names.

## Evidence

The `in` operator traverses the prototype chain, which `Object.hasOwn` (ES2022) does not. This is the correct behavior for lookup tables that should only match explicitly defined keys.

target.title: Restore Telegram /steer for active Codex runs
target.body:
## Summary

- Route Telegram `/steer`, `/tell`, and `/queue` text commands through the Telegram control lane so active-run management does not wait behind the busy chat/topic lane.
- Teach `/steer` active-run lookup to recover from slash-lane targeting by checking the active session key, session file, sibling direct/DM lanes, and then the legacy stored session id fallback.
- Register Codex app-server active runs with their `sessionFile`, matching the generic embedded runner so channel command lookup can find active Codex turns and deliver through existing `turn/steer`.

Fixes #81594.
Refs #48003.
Refs #82479.
Supersedes #81592 and #81613.

## What Problem This Solves

Users sending `/steer` or `/tell` from Telegram during an active Codex run can currently see the command arrive too late or get treated as a separate prompt. That makes channel-native steering unreliable exactly when a user is trying to correct a long-running Codex turn without terminal access.

## Root Cause

Telegram `/steer` text commands had two independent ways to miss an active Codex turn:

1. Telegram sequentialization did not classify `/steer`, `/tell`, or `/queue` as control-lane commands, so they could wait behind the same long-running chat/topic lane they were meant to control.
2. `/steer` active-run resolution stopped after the selected session key and stored session id. If the selected key was a slash lane, or if the active Codex run was only recoverable from the transcript session file, the command fell through as a new prompt instead of steering the active turn.

Codex app-server already supports steering with `turn/steer`; the missing piece was registering the active run with the same `sessionFile` lookup used by the generic embedded runner.

## Flow

```text
Telegram /steer or /tell
  -> Telegram control sequential lane
  -> shared /steer command handler
  -> active run lookup:
       target key
       target session file
       slash sibling direct/DM keys
       sibling session files
       stored active session id
  -> Codex steering queue
  -> app-server turn/steer
```

## Scope

This restores the Telegram command path for active Codex runs. It intentionally does not claim to fix the broader `messages.queue.mode: "steer"` lifecycle/admission regression tracked in #48003, where ordinary non-command messages can still be delayed before they reach queue policy.

## Evidence

- RED checks first:
  - Telegram control-lane tests failed for `/steer`, `/tell`, `/queue`, and targeted command forms.
  - `/steer` resolver tests failed for session-file lookup and slash-lane direct sibling fallback.
  - Codex app-server steering test failed because active runs were not resolvable by session file.
- GREEN:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-steer.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.steering.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Result: 3 test files passed, 63 tests passed.
- Static checks:
  - `git diff --check`
  - Focused `oxlint` on touched files.
  - Focused `oxfmt --check --threads=1` on touched files.
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- Broader type note:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo` currently fails on unrelated `extensions/qa-lab` crabline type errors. The new Codex steering test type error found during development was fixed and did not recur.
- GitNexus:
  - `detect_changes` reports medium risk, with affected processes limited to Telegram dispatch/sequential-key flow.

## Proof Request

After CI starts, please run Telegram visible proof for this PR:

```text
@openclaw-mantis telegram live proof: verify /steer and /tell bypass a busy Telegram Codex run and reach the active Codex turn via turn/steer instead of becoming a delayed follow-up.
```

target.title: docs(codex): document native model lifecycle hooks
target.body:
## Summary

- document Codex native `PreModelRequest` / `PostModelResponse` hooks as Codex-owned command hooks
- clarify that OpenClaw plugin `llm_input` / `llm_output` remain adapter-level compatibility events
- call out that OpenClaw does not double-fire model lifecycle plugin hooks when Codex native hooks are available

## Dependency

- Depends on https://github.com/openai/codex/pull/19182
- Related to https://github.com/openai/codex/pull/19060 because #19182 is expected to stack after or include the compaction hook work

## Validation
