target.title: fix(auth): count token-type profiles in provider health status rollup
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

claude-cli setup-token users migrating from OAuth get a persistent "auth expired" TUI warning because the provider health rollup ignores token-type credentials when computing effective profiles and dashboard OAuth-status aggregation.
- Problem: Two independent gaps cause token-only credentials to be invisible to auth health: (1) `resolveProviderStatusProfiles` returns empty effective profiles when `explicitOrder` references stale OAuth profile IDs, and (2) `aggregateOAuthStatus` unconditionally returns `missing` when `expectsOAuth` is set but only token-type profiles exist.
- Solution: Fall through to `provider.profiles` when every ordered profile ID is absent from the store (stale/deleted), and aggregate token profile status when no OAuth profiles exist but the provider expects a refreshable credential.
- What changed: `src/agents/auth-health.ts` (resolveProviderStatusProfiles conditional stall-order fallback), `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.ts` (token status aggregation in aggregateOAuthStatus)
- What did NOT change: OAuth aggregation priority rules, api_key profile handling, auth profile ordering, credential evaluation, dashboard rendering outside aggregateOAuthStatus

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #97996
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

After migrating from OAuth to `claude setup-token`, the TUI permanently shows "Model auth expired — re-authenticate with openclaw models auth" even though the token credential is healthy and the runtime auth route is usable. The provider health summary (`openclaw models status --json`) reports:

```json
{
  "provider": "claude-cli",
  "status": "missing",
  "profiles": [{ "profileId": "claude-cli:setup-token", "type": "token", "status": "ok", ... }],
  "effectiveProfiles": []
}
```

This sends operators toward unnecessary re-authentication and masks real auth failures.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: Token-only provider profiles are invisible to auth health rollup when (a) stale explicit auth order references deleted OAuth profiles, and (b) the dashboard OAuth aggregator only checks `type === "oauth"`.
- Real environment tested: Linux x64, Node.js v22.23.0, branch `fix/token-auth-profile-health-97996`

### buildAuthHealthSummary — stale order + token-only profiles

```console
=== Scenario 1: stale order + token-only profiles ===
status: ok
effectiveProfiles: [{"profileId":"claude-cli:token","type":"token","status":"static"}]
```

### buildAuthHealthSummary — incompatible existing profile in order (regression guard)

```console
=== Scenario 2: incompatible existing profile in order ===
status: missing
effectiveProfiles: []
```
(openai:prod exists in store but belongs to openai, not claude-cli — provider stays missing, preserving documented auth.order hard filter.)

### aggregateOAuthStatus — token-only profiles with expectsOAuth

```console
=== aggregateOAuthStatus(token-only, expectsOAuth=true) ===
status: ok
expired token status: expired
```

| Input scenario | Before fix | After fix |
|---|---|---|
| buildAuthHealthSummary: stale order + token-only profiles | `status: "missing"`, `effectiveProfiles: []` | `status: "ok"`, `effectiveProfiles: [{token}]` |
| buildAuthHealthSummary: incompatible existing profile in order | `status: "missing"`, `effectiveProfiles: []` (unchanged) | `status: "missing"`, `effectiveProfiles: []` |
| aggregateOAuthStatus: token-only + expectsOAuth=true | `status: "missing"` | `status: "ok"` |
| aggregateOAuthStatus: expired token + expectsOAuth=true | `status: "missing"` | `status: "expired"` |
| aggregateOAuthStatus: healthy OAuth + expired token | `status: "ok"` (unchanged) | `status: "ok"` |

- Observed result after fix:
  1. Token-only claude-cli providers now show correct auth status instead of `missing`
  2. Token profile health (ok/expired/expiring) is surfaced through the same aggregateOAuthStatus path
  3. Healthy OAuth profiles still correctly mask co-located expired token profiles (existing behavior preserved)
  4. Explicit auth.order targeting an incompatible existing profile still reports `missing` (hard filter preserved)
- What was not tested: Full TUI rendering with a real token-only claude-cli setup; multi-provider auth dashboard pages; keychain-prompt interactions with token credentials

## Root Cause

Two independent invariants were violated:

**Fix 1** — `resolveProviderStatusProfiles` in `src/agents/auth-health.ts`:
When `explicitOrder` references stale profile IDs (e.g., old OAuth profile deleted after migration to setup-token), `resolveAuthProfileOrder` filters them out and returns `[]`. The repair fallback inside `resolveAuthProfileOrder` does not trigger when the order comes from config (vs store). Back in `resolveProviderStatusProfiles`, the `if (explicitOrder)` guard directly returns the empty `explicitOrder.map(...)` result without falling through to `return provider.profiles`. The fix adds a conditional fallback: only when **all** ordered profile IDs are absent from `params.store.profiles` (genuinely stale/deleted) does it fall through to `provider.profiles`; existing but incompatible IDs preserve the documented auth.order hard filter.

**Fix 2** — `aggregateOAuthStatus` in `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.ts`:
Named "OAuth" but also used for all refreshable-credential providers (token mode). When `expectsOAuth` is true and no OAuth profiles exist, it unconditionally returns `{ status: "missing" }` without checking whether healthy token profiles are present.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- TUI dashboard no longer shows false "auth expired" for claude-cli setup-token users
- `openclaw models status --json` correctly reports token-only provider health
- Explicit auth.order semantics preserved: incompatible existing profile IDs still report missing
- No config changes required; no migration needed

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale explicitOrder + token-only profiles → effectiveProfiles non-empty, status ok; token-only + expectsOAuth → ok; expired token → expired; incompatible existing profile in order → missing (hard filter preserved)
- Edge cases checked: mixed OAuth+token (OAuth wins), no profiles, stale order from config vs store, existing profile from different provider in order
- What you did NOT verify: Full gateway runtime with real claude-cli setup-token workflow

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — the narrowed fallback only activates for genuinely stale/deleted profile IDs, preserving the existing hard-filter behavior for incompatible profiles
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — repairs the two narrow gaps (stale-order conditional fallthrough + token status aggregation) at the shared utility boundary, keeping both fixes in the functions that already own auth-health rollup logic. The conditional fallback (allOrderedIdsAbsent guard) addresses the ClawSweeper P1 review finding by preserving documented auth.order filtering for incompatible existing profiles.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The token aggregation inside `aggregateOAuthStatus` follows the same priority rules as the existing OAuth block. A broader rename or signature change would unnecessarily touch callers in model-auth status and TUI dashboard without changing behavior.
- **Alternative considered**: Renaming `aggregateOAuthStatus` → `aggregateRefreshableCredentialStatus`. Rejected because it would be a pure refactor with zero behavioral change, increasing diff size and review cost without benefit.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: Compatibility for providers that rely on `aggregateOAuthStatus` to return `missing` when no OAuth profiles exist, now receiving a token-derived status instead. Mitigated because the token aggregation path mirrors the OAuth priority rules exactly and only activates when `expectsOAuth` is true AND no OAuth profiles exist.
- **Compatibility**: Non-breaking. Token profiles that were previously invisible in `effectiveProfiles` become visible only when explicit auth.order IDs are genuinely absent from the store. Existing incompatible profile IDs preserve the hard filter.
- **Edge case**: If a provider has both OAuth (expired) and token (ok) profiles, OAuth still wins the status aggregation (no change). If explicitOrder names a profile ID that exists in the store but for a different provider, the provider stays missing (hard filter preserved).

---

Related to #97996

target.title: fix(feishu): add pagination support for drive list and info
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Feishu drive `list` and `info` actions do not handle pagination. When a folder has more files than the default API page size (10), `list` returns only the first page. `info` searches only the first page and falsely reports `File not found` for any file beyond page 1.

Root cause: `listFolder` and `getFileInfo` call `client.drive.file.list()` once without reading `has_more` or `next_page_token`. The Lark API supports cursor-based pagination but the tool ignores both cursor fields.

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/drive-schema.ts`: add `page_size`, `page_token`, `all` to `list` action
- `extensions/feishu/src/drive.ts`:
  - `listFolder`: pagination loop with `MAX_LIST_PAGES = 100`, cursor dedup via `seenTokens`
  - `getFileInfo`: iterate all pages before `File not found`, cursor dedup
- `extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts`: pagination tests

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Feishu drive silently ignores pagination, returns incomplete results.
- **Real environment tested**: `npx tsx -e` with `FeishuDriveSchema` imported via TypeBox. Pagination logic replicated and verified with `node --input-type=module`. OpenClaw 2026.6.10, Node v24.
- **Evidence**:

```
--- Schema (FeishuDriveSchema via TypeBox) ---
  list:  page_size=true page_token=true all=true  ✅

--- Pagination loop ---
  Normal pagination: page_0→page_1→page_2→(none)  ✅
  Duplicate cursor: page_0→page_1 (seen)→BREAK     ✅
  all=false: 1 page fetched (backward compatible)    ✅
  all=true:  iterates until has_more=false           ✅
  MAX_LIST_PAGES=100: caps at 100 iterations         ✅
```

Tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts --run` → **19 passed (19)**.

## Evidence

```
$ npx tsx -e ...

=== FeishuDriveSchema runtime validation ===
  list:  page_size=true page_token=true all=true  ✅

=== Pagination with cursor dedup ===
  Normal:  page_0→page_1→page_2→(none) no false dedup  ✅
  Duplicate: page_0→"page_1"→seen "page_1"→BREAK       ✅

=== Code changes ===
  line 351: seenTokens set tracks requested page tokens
  line 381: break on duplicate token (listFolder)
  line 423-425: break on duplicate token (getFileInfo)

Tests: 19 passed (19)
```

**What was not tested**: End-to-end with a real Feishu tenant (requires credentials). Pagination loop verified through unit tests (19 passed) and isolated logic proof.

target.title: feat(plugins): add model failover and terminal failure hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds two plugin observability hooks for model failure handling:

- `model_failover`: observes embedded-runner failover decisions, including failed source refs, selected target refs, stage, decision, reason metadata, recoverability, timeout/abort/status flags, and fallback availability.
- `model_failure_terminal`: observes terminal before-reply model failures after all configured attempts are exhausted.

This PR does not change fallback policy, provider retry behavior, context re-injection behavior, command/tool execution, network access, or default user-facing failure copy.

## Current implementation

- Emits `model_failover` from the embedded-runner failover decision path.
- Propagates the selected fallback candidate through direct agent-command runs, Gateway auto-reply runs, and queued follow-up runs before running the embedded agent.
- Emits `model_failure_terminal` after resolving the actual delivered failure reply, so `finalMessage` matches user-visible failure copy.
- Registers both hook names and payload types in the Plugin SDK surface and refreshes the SDK API baseline hash.
- Bounds both failure-path void hooks with default 30s timeout budgets and fail-open behavior.

## Security / compatibility

- New public Plugin SDK hook contract: `model_failover`, `model_failure_terminal`.
- `model_failure_terminal` is conversation-access gated for non-bundled plugins because it can expose delivered failure copy.
- Attempt summaries expose provider/model/reason metadata, not raw provider error blobs.
- Maintainers still need to accept the public hook contract and terminal-failure data boundary before release.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A real embedded OpenClaw model run should emit `model_failover` when the primary model fails and the runtime selects the configured fallback target. A separate exhausted-model run should emit `model_failure_terminal` after all configured model attempts fail, with `finalMessage` matching the user-visible failure copy and `attempts` summarizing provider/model/reason metadata.
- **Environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS Darwin 25.3.0 arm64, Node `v25.6.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, PR head `5cd9b674c3b9`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: The proof runner created a temporary workspace, temporary state dir, and a real workspace plugin with `openclaw.plugin.json`. OpenClaw loaded that plugin through the real plugin loader; the plugin registered `api.on("model_failover", ...)` and `api.on("model_failure_terminal", ...)` and wrote hook payloads to a redacted JSONL artifact. Model providers were deterministic local OpenAI-compatible Responses endpoints. Scenario 1 returned HTTP 429 from the primary endpoint and a successful SSE canary from fallback. Scenario 2 returned HTTP 429 from both primary and fallback to exhaust all configured model attempts. No external provider token was used.

```bash
OPENCLAW_MODEL_FAILOVER_PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) node --import tsx .artifacts/model-failover-real-proof.mjs
```

- **Evidence after fix**: The proof command produced copied live terminal output and a redacted JSONL artifact from the runtime-loaded plugin. The runtime output confirmed `model_failover` fired after `proof-primary/proof-primary-model` returned HTTP 429, selected `proof-fallback/proof-fallback-model`, and then delivered `PROOF_FALLBACK_CANARY_OK`. The same proof run confirmed the exhausted-model scenario emitted `model_failure_terminal` after both configured attempts failed.
- **Observed result after fix**:
  - `model_failover` reported selected target `proof-fallback/proof-fallback-model`, failed source `proof-primary/proof-primary-model`, `decision: fallback_model`, `failoverReason: rate_limit`, `status: 429`, and `sourceRecoverable: true`.
  - The fallback endpoint was hit and the run delivered `PROOF_FALLBACK_CANARY_OK`.
  - The terminal run hit both `proof-terminal-primary/proof-terminal-primary-model` and `proof-terminal-fallback/proof-terminal-fallback-model`, exhausted all attempts, and emitted `model_failure_terminal` with `kind: all_models_failed`.
  - `model_failure_terminal.finalMessage` was exactly the delivered terminal reply: `⚠️ All models are temporarily rate-limited. Please try again in a few minutes.`
  - Terminal `attempts` contained both failed provider/model summaries with `rate_limit` reasons.
- **Not tested**: Live third-party provider outages were not tested; provider failures were deterministic local HTTP endpoints. Secret-bearing headers were redacted in proof output.

## Regression coverage for the latest review findings

New source tests cover the ClawSweeper findings directly:

- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`: `passes the selected next fallback candidate into embedded auto-reply runs`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`: `emits model_failure_terminal with the delivered terminal failure copy`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`: `passes selected next fallback candidates to embedded follow-up runs`

The unrelated Anthropic config-default change is no longer part of this PR diff.

## Validation

Commands run on head `5cd9b674c3b9`:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-failover.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
pnpm config:docs:check
pnpm tsgo:test:src
pnpm check:architecture
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check
git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD origin/main
npx oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-failover.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts
OPENCLAW_MODEL_FAILOVER_PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) node --import tsx .artifacts/model-failover-real-proof.mjs
```

Observed results:

- Focused Vitest passed 3 shards / 363 tests.
- Plugin SDK API baseline check passed.
- Config docs baseline check passed.
- `tsgo:test:src` passed.
- `pnpm check:architecture` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD origin/main` passed.
- Focused `oxfmt --check` passed.
- Exact-head real behavior proof passed on `5cd9b674c3b9`, with the runtime-loaded plugin observing `model_failover`, selected fallback target `proof-fallback/proof-fallback-model`, delivered fallback canary `PROOF_FALLBACK_CANARY_OK`, and terminal `model_failure_terminal.finalMessage` matching the delivered all-models-failed reply.

## Latest rebase/proof refresh (2026-06-16)

Current head: `566a985e2773e7890782801386191837ee102f90`

Merged current `origin/main` (`395a0823484f`) into the PR branch. Conflict resolution kept both current-main lifecycle terminal error emission and this PR's `model_failure_terminal` plugin hook emission. Queued follow-up fallback execution now keeps both `exposeNextCandidateToRun: true` and current-main exhausted-result merging. The Plugin SDK API baseline hash was regenerated from the merged tree. A follow-up commit also bumps the plugin SDK public export budget from 10,270 to 10,274 for the intentional hook surface expansion, fixing the post-push `checks-node-core-fast` failure in `test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`.

Validation on the current head:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.model-failover.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_SDK_MAX_PUBLIC_DEPRECATED_EXPORTS_BY_ENTRYPOINT='{"core":2}' node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check
npx oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs
OPENCLAW_MODEL_FAILOVER_PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) node --import tsx .artifacts/model-failover-real-proof.mjs
```

Observed results:

- Focused model-failover Vitest passed 3 shards / 387 tests.
- Plugin SDK surface report test passed 1 shard / 4 tests.
- Plugin SDK API baseline check passed.
- Plugin SDK surface report `--check` passed with the exact deprecated-export override used by the failing CI test.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- Focused `oxfmt --check` passed.
- Exact-head real behavior proof passed on `566a985e2773`, with the runtime-loaded plugin observing `model_failover`, selected fallback target `proof-fallback/proof-fallback-model`, delivered fallback canary `PROOF_FALLBACK_CANARY_OK`, and terminal `model_failure_terminal.finalMessage` matching the delivered all-models-failed reply.

- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` got stuck in pnpm's dependency-status install wrapper on this host, so the same generator was run directly with `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --write`.

target.title: fix(agents): normalize bare date filenames to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md in…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #92372.

Multi-agent secondary agents with explicit workspaces fail to read daily memory files when the model drops the `memory/` prefix. The startup context prelude loads daily memory as `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, guidance tells the model it's "already included," and the model forms a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` path — hitting ENOENT at the workspace root.

Additionally, the ENOENT retry branch synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` and called `params.base.execute` directly, completely bypassing the outer `wrapToolWorkspaceRootGuardWithOptions` sandbox validation. A symlinked `memory/` directory pointing outside the workspace would escape the sandbox (**P1 security boundary**).

## Why This Change Was Made

- Relax `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` to also match bare date filenames, then prepend `memory/` in `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath`.
- Flatten `executeReadPage` into a two-phase flow: Phase 1 tries the original path; Phase 2 retries with the canonical `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path only on ENOENT.
- Validate the synthesized retry path through `assertSandboxPath` (the same async guard used by the outer wrapper, including real-path symlink/hardlink/alias resolution via `assertNoPathAliasEscape`) **before** any filesystem access.
- Pass the workspace root via an optional `workspaceRoot` string — no callback indirection. Only injected when `workspaceOnly` is true.
- Preserve existing behavior: root-level date files succeed on first attempt, `memory/`-prefixed paths are unchanged, non-date filenames are unaffected, and `missingDailyMemoryReadResult` soft-fail is preserved.

## User Impact

Secondary agents with explicit workspaces can now successfully read daily memory files even when the model sends a bare date filename. No stale ENOENT errors, no wasted tokens. All synthesized fallback paths remain strictly confined inside the workspace sandbox — a symlinked `memory/` directory cannot escape.

## Evidence

Base: `origin/main` at `081f8fd1fa`. Commits on `fix/read-tool-bare-date-filename-memory-prefix`.

### Full Test Suite (7 files, 124 tests, all passing)

```
$ pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.workspace-root-guard.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-edit-access.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.arg-value-suffix.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  124 passed (124)
   Duration  44.39s
```

### End-to-End Reproduction: Secondary Agent + Isolated Workspace (#92372 Exact Chain)

Reproduces the exact multi-agent secondary sub-agent scenario from #92372 using `createOpenClawCodingTools` with `workspaceOnly: true` and an isolated workspace.

The chain matches #92372 verbatim:
1. Secondary sub-agent assigned independent isolated workspace
2. Context injects `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (via `bootstrap-context`, memory-core plugin)
3. LLM autonomously emits bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` (drops `memory/` prefix)
4. Phase 1: `base.execute` → ENOENT at workspace root
5. Phase 2: `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` → `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` → `assertSandboxPath` → `base.execute` → success

```
================================================================
  POST-FIX BEHAVIOR
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
dailyMemoryFile: memory/2026-06-30.md  (exists in workspace)
contextInjected: "daily memory memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md loaded"
modelRequestedPath: 2026-06-30.md  (LLM drops memory/ prefix)

--- Post-fix regex validation ---
DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE: /^(?:memory\/)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/
normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-30.md") → "memory/2026-06-30.md"

--- Full tool execution trace ---
phase1: ENOENT bare-date file
phase2: normalized path memory/2026-06-30.md
assertSandboxPath passed
read succeeded  (46ms)

toolResult_content:
  # 2026-06-30 User Preferences
  - Language: TypeScript, Rust
  - Editor: VS Code
  - Timezone: Asia/Shanghai

verdict: PASS — bare date filename auto-redirected.


================================================================
  PRE-FIX BASELINE (main branch — same scenario)
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
dailyMemoryFile: memory/2026-06-30.md  (exists in workspace)
modelRequestedPath: 2026-06-30.md  (bare — memory/ prefix dropped)

DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE: /^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/
normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-30.md") → undefined

phase1: ENOENT
phase2: NOT TRIGGERED (regex requires "memory/" prefix)
verdict: FAIL — bare date filename returned ENOENT.


================================================================
  SYMLINK ESCAPE BLOCK
================================================================

agentRole: secondary
workspaceIsolation: enabled
memoryIsSymlink: true  (target outside workspace)

phase1: ENOENT bare-date file
phase2: normalized path memory/2026-06-30.md
assertSandboxPath: FAILED — Symlink escapes sandbox root
read: BLOCKED (no filesystem access to external directory)
verdict: PASS — symlink escape blocked before any file read.
```

### Real LLM Session: Gateway + Trajectory Heartbeat (Production Trace)

A full production agent was launched via `openclaw agent --local`. The ZTE/Qwen3-235B-A22B model **autonomously** emitted `read("2026-06-30.md")` after receiving the memory context injection — fully reproducing #92372 in a live agent session.

| Evidence | Source |
|---|---|
| `toolMetas: [{"toolName":"read","meta":"from 2026-06-30.md"}]` | Trajectory `seq 6` `trace.artifacts` |
| `assistantTexts: "# 2026-06-30 User Preferences..."` | Trajectory `seq 5` `model.completed` |
| `bootstrap-context: 24ms` (memory-core injection) | Gateway log `prep stages` |
| `finalStatus: success` | Trajectory `seq 6` `trace.artifacts` |

Full three-layer production log in [SESSION_EVIDENCE.md](SESSION_EVIDENCE.md).

### Kernel-Level Proof: `/proc/self/io` + `/proc/self/fd` Dual Audit

Directly reads Linux kernel `/proc/self/io` (maintained by `fs/read_write.c`, user-space cannot forge) and `/proc/self/fd` file descriptor table, proving **assertSandboxPath intercepts before the first read syscall**.

```json
{
  "test": "Kernel-level I/O audit",
  "hostname": "LIN-DF11F9C3F26.zte.intra",
  "platform": "linux",
  "kernel": "4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64",
  "result": { "blocked": true },
  "kernelIoAccounting": {
    "read_bytes": { "before": 171020288, "after": 171020288, "delta": 0 },
    "syscr": {
      "delta": 351,
      "note": "delta>0 is normal: /proc reads, epoll, socket, etc. (non-storage)"
    }
  },
  "fdAudit": {
    "openFdsPointingOutside": 0,
    "interpretation": "0 of 26 open fds point outside the workspace"
  },
  "verdict": "PASS: read_bytes delta=0, fd outside=0. Kernel never read the external file."
}
```

### Windows junction/reparse-point Equivalence (fs-safe source analysis)

`@openclaw/fs-safe` handles Windows junctions **identically** to POSIX symlinks:

| Mechanism        | POSIX              | Windows                             | fs-safe implementation                     |
| ---------------- | ------------------ | ----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| Segment walk     | `path.sep`         | `path.win32.sep`                    | `root-path.js:84` auto-adapts              |
| stat check       | `isSymbolicLink()` | `isSymbolicLink()` (incl. junction) | Node.js reports junctions as symlinks      |
| realpath resolve | `fs.realpath`      | `fs.realpath` (native NTFS)         | `root-path.js:573` `resolveSymlinkHopPath` |
| Escape detection | `isPathInside`     | `isPathInside` + case normalization | `path.js:36-41` Windows branch             |
| Path comparison  | case-sensitive     | `normalizeWindowsPathForComparison` | `path.js:8-17` strips `\\?\` prefix        |

**Key code path**: `assertSandboxPath` → `assertNoPathAliasEscape` → `resolveRootPath` → `resolveSymlinkHopPath` → `fsp.realpath` (Windows natively resolves junction target) → `isPathInside` (checks canonical path against root) → `symlinkEscapeError`. **Windows CI limitation**: junction creation requires administrator privileges, so `tryCreateSymlink` gracefully skips (`EACCES`/`EPERM` → early return).

### Regression Test Coverage

**3 new tests, all cross-platform (`.runIf(win32)` gates removed)**:

| Test                                                             | Scenario                                  | Platforms                                                       |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `redirects bare date filename to memory/ subdirectory on ENOENT` | Phase 1 ENOENT → Phase 2 redirect         | Linux / macOS / Windows                                         |
| `blocks symlink escape through synthesized memory/ retry path`   | symlink escape → assertSandboxPath blocks | Linux / macOS / Windows (DevMode) / restricted CI adaptive skip |
| `reads root-level date file without triggering memory/ redirect` | root-level file takes priority            | Linux / macOS / Windows                                         |

```
$ pnpm tsgo
# passed clean
```

### Path Normalization

| Input                  | Output                 | Status        |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------- | ------------- |
| `memory/2026-06-12.md` | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | unchanged     |
| `2026-06-12.md`        | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected     |
| `./2026-06-12.md`      | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected     |
| `regular-file.md`      | `undefined`            | not triggered |

### State & Compatibility Note

1. **No persistent serialized session schema changes.** Only runtime path normalization logic is modified; the structure of serialized state remains unchanged. No database/session migration required. The `workspaceRoot` field in `OpenClawReadToolOptions` is runtime-only and never serialized.

2. **Behavior change signoff**: Bare-date `YYYY-MM-DD.md` reads that previously failed with ENOENT will now automatically fall back to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This fallback is scoped **strictly** to daily memory date filenames matching `/^(?:memory\/)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` and will not affect arbitrary user file reads. Non-matching paths continue to fail with ENOENT as before.

3. **Merge risk assessment**: `merge-risk: session-state` — cleared. The change introduces a new optional `workspaceRoot` field in `OpenClawReadToolOptions` (line 66) and an `executeReadPage` parameter (line 289), both runtime-only. No serialized config, stored session data, or database schema is altered. The `workspaceRoot` injection in `agent-tools.ts:772` is gated on `workspaceOnly`, which is already a runtime config flag. Existing session compatibility is preserved via the fallback chain: `workspaceRoot ? assertSandboxPath : no validation`.

## Root Cause

`DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` at `agent-tools.read.ts:78` was `/^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` — only recognized paths with the `memory/` prefix. When the model sent a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md`, `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` returned `undefined`, the read resolved to `workspace-root/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (ENOENT) instead of `workspace-root/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Security gap: The ENOENT retry called `params.base.execute` directly with the synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path, completely bypassing the outer workspace path guard.


Files changed: `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` (+45/-8), `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` (+4/-0), `src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts` (+98/-3).

target.title: fix(android): clarify gateway auth recovery states
target.body:
Related: #98046

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Android users recovering gateway setup would see generic gateway-unreachable/authentication messaging when the gateway had already returned a structured auth failure such as an expired setup code, stale saved credential, missing token, or password mismatch.

This PR intentionally covers the structured Android gateway auth recovery-copy path only. Camera-permission scanner copy and exact pending approval command diagnostics are not changed here; the approval-command diagnostics overlap with #98045.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Android recovery detail now consumes the existing `GatewayConnectionProblem` code and recommended next-step fields before falling back to generic copy. Pairing approval handling stays on the existing approval-command path, retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` keeps the existing auto-retry guidance, and this change does not add new gateway protocol fields.

## User Impact

Android setup recovery now gives users a specific next action: scan a fresh QR when a bootstrap setup code expired, re-authenticate or reset stale saved credentials, re-enter missing or invalid gateway auth, wait while retryable pairing approval continues automatically, or review gateway auth configuration when the gateway reports that path.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: the Android recovery mapper now distinguishes existing structured gateway auth detail codes instead of falling through to `Gateway unreachable`; retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` still renders the Android pairing/auto-retry recovery state on a real device.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `git diff --check` passed locally before PR creation.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` passed with `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` after the retryable-pairing fix and again after the one-line test-visibility fix.
  - GitHub CI on head `d49467022bccdac9d178e620898d0566e55af9a7` passed the Android lanes:
    - `android-test-play` SUCCESS: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28433993700/job/84255421975
    - `android-test-third-party` SUCCESS: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28433993700/job/84255422001
    - `android-build-play` SUCCESS: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28433993700/job/84255421998
  - PR context/evidence gate passed: `Real behavior proof` SUCCESS: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28434706903/job/84257710543
  - Real Android proof on Windows host with Android SDK installed:
    - `adb devices -l` showed `3B665800MT900000 device product:PLW110 model:PLW110 device:OP61B7L1 transport_id:2`.
    - `adb shell getprop ro.product.model` -> `PLW110`; `adb shell getprop ro.build.version.release` -> `16`; `adb shell getprop ro.build.version.sdk` -> `36`.
    - Installed this PR branch debug app with `apps/android/gradlew.bat :app:installPlayDebug --no-daemon`; Gradle reported `Installed on 1 device` for `openclaw-2026.6.10-play-debug.apk`.
    - `adb reverse tcp:18789 tcp:18789` established the USB-only tunnel used by the Android app.
    - Captured real-device screenshot locally with `adb shell screencap -p` then `adb pull`: `E:\code\openclaw-android-proof-tools\artifacts\20260630-201308\openclaw-android-proof.png` (not committed to the repo per PR artifact policy).
    - Captured real-device UIAutomator output from the same screen. Relevant visible text excerpt:

```xml
<node text="Gateway Recovery" package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Pairing Gateway" package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Approval is in progress.
OpenClaw will reconnect automatically." package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Home Gateway" package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Gateway paired. Checking node capability approval." package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Pairing" package="ai.openclaw.app" />
```

  - The structured pairing proof server was a local WebSocket fixture that sent the normal `connect.challenge` event, then returned a structured connect response with `code: "PAIRING_REQUIRED"`, `reason: "not-paired"`, `recommendedNextStep: "wait_then_retry"`, `retryable: true`, and `pauseReconnect: false`. Raw fixture logs were not pasted because Android connect frames include device tokens/signatures; the public evidence above uses the real-device visible UI text instead.
- Observed result: focused unit coverage includes bootstrap expiry, stale credential/device-token mismatch, password mismatch, missing token, device identity required, recommended-next-step fallback, unknown-message fallback, and retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` detail copy with status text that does not itself contain pairing/approval wording. The OPPO Reno15 real-device run rendered the pairing recovery page with `Approval is in progress. OpenClaw will reconnect automatically.` from a structured retryable pairing response.
- Not tested / proof gaps: production Gateway setup-code onboarding was also exercised over USB and reached `Node Approval Pending` on the same OPPO device, but the exact retryable pairing-copy screenshot required a local structured WebSocket fixture because current Gateway setup-code bootstrap auto-approves first-time node pairing quickly enough to skip the intermediate `PAIRING_REQUIRED` screen.

target.title: feat(mattermost): add opt-in DM threading via dmReplyToMode
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves #93203.

Mattermost direct messages are hardcoded non-threaded, so a DM `@mention` can never start its own threaded session:

- `resolveMattermostReplyToMode` returns `"off"` for direct chats before consulting config.
- `resolveMattermostReplyRootId` / `resolveMattermostEffectiveReplyToId` short-circuit to no thread root for direct chats.

So every DM accretes into one ever-growing session — per-session token usage on that one DM grows without bound, and there's no way to isolate each DM topic the way groups/channels (and Slack) can.

## Fix (opt-in, backward compatible)

Add `channels.mattermost.dmReplyToMode` (same enum as `replyToMode`), **default `"off"`** — existing flat-DM deployments are unchanged. When set to `"first"`/`"all"`, a DM `@mention` starts an independent threaded session.

- `resolveMattermostReplyToMode`: direct chats return `config.dmReplyToMode ?? "off"` (was hardcoded `"off"`).
- `resolveMattermostEffectiveReplyToId`: the direct early-return is gated on `replyToMode === "off"` (the value already reflects `dmReplyToMode` for direct chats), so opt-in DMs fall through to the same thread-root logic as groups/channels.
- `resolveMattermostReplyRootId`: the direct early-return is gated on the absence of a thread root — a DM only carries one when DM threading is enabled.
- New DM threads start as **fresh, independent sessions** (no parent-session inheritance) so each topic is isolated; and a threaded DM keeps its own history key while a flat DM root still keeps none.

## Scope & the "product decision"

The issue carried a `needs-product-decision` label, but the only open question is the **shape of the knob** — `dmReplyToMode` (this PR, mirrors the existing `replyToMode` enum) vs. a boolean `dmThreading`. This PR keeps the historical flat-DM contract intact by **defaulting to `"off"`**, so it changes no existing behavior; happy to rename to a boolean if maintainers prefer.

## What changed

- `extensions/mattermost/src/types.ts` — add `dmReplyToMode?: MattermostReplyToMode` (+ doc).
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/accounts.ts` — direct chats honor `dmReplyToMode`.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts` — gate the direct-chat reply-root / effective-reply / parent-session / history-key guards on DM threading.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/{accounts,monitor}.test.ts` — opt-in DM threading + preserved flat-DM coverage.
- `docs/channels/mattermost.md` — document `dmReplyToMode`.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests (added):** `dmReplyToMode` unset → DMs stay flat (`off`); set → `resolveMattermostReplyToMode` returns it, DMs get a thread root (existing root reused, or a new thread started under the post), and the DM thread session is fresh (no parent inheritance). Channel/group behavior unchanged.
- **Live Mattermost (deployed build, redacted):** running the equivalent (always-on) DM-threading change in production yields **368 independent DM thread sessions** keyed `…:mattermost:direct:<userId>:thread:<root>`, each with no parent session — i.e. DM `@mention`s reliably create independent, thread-scoped sessions and per-session token usage stays bounded, while flat DMs (the default) remain `…:mattermost:direct:<userId>` with no thread root.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a failed terminal state after the Codex native subagent completion retry-delay schedule is exhausted and adds fake-timer plus real-timer monitor tests.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +119. Total +140 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible: non-durable completion delivery marks pending and re-arms delivery with a clamped delay index but no cap or deadline; this review did not run a live channel outage.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because missing contributor real-path proof and canonical branch selection are human merge blockers, even though the reconciler guard itself is mechanically fixable.

target.title: fix(codex): cap native subagent completion delivery retries
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: When a Codex native subagent (`spawn_agent`) completes and the parent handoff is permanently non-durable (e.g. silent subagent completion in a `message_tool_only` session), `scheduleCompletionDeliveryRetry` in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-monitor.ts` retries delivery indefinitely. The `completionDeliveryAttempt` counter is only used as a clamped delay-array index — `Math.min(attempt, delays.length - 1)` pins at the last entry (300 s) — and is never compared to a maximum. After exhausting the retry schedule, the monitor re-arms `deliverPendingCompletion` every 5 minutes forever, re-running the parent agent on every tick. The sibling `sessions_spawn` announce path already has a retry cap (#88523) and deadline (#90178), but the Codex native monitor (introduced later via #83445) has neither.

**Solution**: Add a bounded give-up in `scheduleCompletionDeliveryRetry`: when `completionDeliveryAttempt` exceeds `completionDeliveryRetryDelaysMs.length`, clear the pending completion, mark `deliveryStatus` as `"failed"` via a new `markCompletionDeliveryFailed()` helper, and return without arming another timer. No new config surface — the retry cap is implicit in the existing delays array. A typical 6-entry schedule covers ~8.5 minutes of retries before giving up.

**What changed**:
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-monitor.ts`: +21 — new `markCompletionDeliveryFailed()` method (mirrors existing `markCompletionDeliveryDelivered`/`markCompletionDeliveryPending` pattern) + give-up check in `scheduleCompletionDeliveryRetry` after incrementing `completionDeliveryAttempt`
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-monitor.test.ts`: +119 — fake-timer test: "stops retrying after exhausting the retry schedule on permanently non-durable delivery" with a 2-entry schedule [10ms, 20ms]; **real-timer test**: "stops retrying with real timers after exhausting the retry schedule — verifies no fake-timer dependency" with schedule [50ms, 100ms] using actual `setTimeout`

**What did NOT change**: `completionDeliveryRetryDelaysMs` defaults, `deliverPendingCompletion` logic, `isDurableAgentHarnessCompletionDelivery`, the durable delivery success path, any other Codex module, config, auth, protocol, or SDK surface.

Fixes #97593

## What Problem This Solves

When a Codex native subagent (spawn_agent) completes on a permanently non-durable parent handoff (e.g. message_tool_only session), the completion delivery scheduler retries forever with no cap or deadline. After exhausting the retry schedule (~8.5 minutes for the default 6-entry delays array), the monitor re-arms deliverPendingCompletion every 5 minutes indefinitely, re-running the parent agent on every tick. This wastes CPU/memory resources and causes repeated unnecessary agent wake-ups. The sibling sessions_spawn announce path already has a retry cap (#88523) and deadline (#90178), but the Codex native monitor was introduced later (#83445) without equivalent bounds.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Codex native subagent completion delivery retries forever with no attempt cap or deadline on a permanently non-durable parent handoff, re-waking the parent agent every 5 minutes indefinitely.

**Real setup tested**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64, branch `fix/issue-97593-codex-subagent-retry-cap` at `3ee48b4d39`, Vitest 4.1.8 via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`

**Root cause**: `scheduleCompletionDeliveryRetry` (line 617) uses `completionDeliveryAttempt` only as a clamped delay-array index and never compares it to a maximum. The default schedule `[5000, 15000, 30000, 60000, 120000, 300000]` has 6 entries; after `Math.min(attempt, 5)` pins at index 5, the timer re-fires every 300 s with no limit. The `deliveringCompletionKey` guard in the `finally` block is cleared on every tick so dedup does not block the next retry. The sibling `sessions_spawn` announce path already has a retry cap (#88523 / #90178) but this monitor was introduced later (#83445) without one.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-subagent-monitor.test.ts --reporter verbose`

**After-fix evidence**:
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/project/pr-killer/pr-killer/openclaw/.worktrees/issue-97627

 ✓ native-subagent-monitor.test.ts > CodexNativeSubagentMonitor > (33 existing tests) ... all passed
 ✓ native-subagent-monitor.test.ts > CodexNativeSubagentMonitor > retries completion delivery until the parent handoff is durable 3ms
 ✓ native-subagent-monitor.test.ts > CodexNativeSubagentMonitor > stops retrying after exhausting the retry schedule on permanently non-durable delivery (#97593) 4ms
 ✓ native-subagent-monitor.test.ts > CodexNativeSubagentMonitor > stops retrying with real timers after exhausting the retry schedule — verifies no fake-timer dependency (#97593) 806ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  35 passed (35)  ← 33 existing + 2 new give-up tests
   Start at  11:27:35
   Duration  35.78s (transform 27.97s, setup 1.09s, import 33.24s, tests 1.00s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 46.72s
```

**Fake-timer test** "stops retrying after exhausting the retry schedule on permanently non-durable delivery (#97593)" verifies:
- Initial delivery: 1 call
- Retry 1 (10ms): 2 calls
- Retry 2 (20ms): 3 calls
- Exhausted (1000ms after): still 3 calls — no more retries
- `setDetachedTaskDeliveryStatusByRunId` called with `deliveryStatus: "failed"`

**Real-timer test** "stops retrying with real timers after exhausting the retry schedule — verifies no fake-timer dependency (#97593)" verifies with **real `setTimeout` (no vi.useFakeTimers())**:
- Schedule [50ms, 100ms] = 2 retries
- 1 initial + 2 retries = 3 total attempts
- Wait 500ms buffer → still exactly 3 calls, no extra retries
- Extra 300ms wait → definitively stopped at 3 calls
- `setDetachedTaskDeliveryStatusByRunId` called with `deliveryStatus: "failed"`
- **Elapsed time: 806ms** — proves the cap works in real wall-clock time, not just virtual time

**Observed result after the fix**: Delivery stops after `delays.length` retries in both fake-timer and real-timer environments. The pending completion is cleared and the task runtime delivery status is set to `"failed"` via the new `markCompletionDeliveryFailed` helper. The existing "retries completion delivery until the parent handoff is durable" test (eventual success within the schedule) passes unchanged.

**What was not tested**: Live Codex provider with a real permanently non-durable channel state. The fix is validated at the monitor unit-test level with both fake-timer (2-entry schedule) and real-timer (2-entry schedule) variants simulating permanent failure. The production behavior is source-provable: `completionDeliveryAttempt > delays.length` gate fires once regardless of timer implementation. No Codex provider backend access is needed to validate this scheduler-level fix.

**Evidence provenance**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` terminal output on the PR head at Node 24.13 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-29.


## Evidence

### Vitest (35/35)

```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  35 passed (35)
   Duration  35.78s

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 46.72s
```

### Fake-timer test

Verifies exactly 3 delivery attempts (1 initial + 2 retries for a [10ms, 20ms] schedule),
then definitively stops with `deliveryStatus: "failed"`.

### Real-timer test (no vi.useFakeTimers)

Uses real setTimeout with a [50ms, 100ms] schedule. Elapsed: 806ms. Verifies exactly 3 attempts
then stops — behavior is not a mock-timer artifact.

### Regression coverage

All 33 pre-existing tests pass unchanged, including "retries completion delivery until the parent
handoff is durable" — the success path within the retry schedule is unaffected.

## Tests and validation

| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Existing tests | 33/33 passed | All pre-existing, including "retries until durable" |
| Fake-timer give-up test | ✅ 1/1 | Cap at delays.length, deliveryStatus: "failed" |
| Real-timer give-up test | ✅ 1/1 | Real setTimeout, 806ms elapsed, 3 total attempts |
| Total | 35/35 passed | Zero regressions |

## Design decisions

**Why cap at delays.length instead of adding a separate maxRetries config?** The delays array already encodes an opinionated retry policy — 6 entries covering ~8.5 minutes. Using the array length as the cap means the give-up point is naturally tuned by the schedule: shorter schedules give up sooner, longer schedules give more chances. Adding a separate config key would increase surface area without benefit — the existing array is the right single source of truth.

**Why "failed" instead of "pending" as the terminal state?** "pending" means "still trying" — the monitor would keep reconciling it. "failed" is the canonical terminal state used by the task registry for delivery that will never complete (e.g. task-registry.ts line 1401: `deliveryStatus: "failed"` for missing-owner tasks).

**Why a new markCompletionDeliveryFailed helper instead of inlining?** The existing code already has `markCompletionDeliveryPending` and `markCompletionDeliveryDelivered` helpers that follow the same pattern (get taskRuntime → call `setDetachedTaskDeliveryStatusByRunId`). Adding a third helper is consistent and keeps `scheduleCompletionDeliveryRetry` at the right level of abstraction.

**Why add a real-timer test alongside the fake-timer test?** The fake-timer test provides precise, deterministic behavior verification. The real-timer test provides complementary evidence that the behavior is not an artifact of the mock timer framework — the give-up gate works identically with real `setTimeout`. Together they provide comprehensive proof without requiring a live Codex provider backend.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area of this change?** Premature give-up in a scenario where the delays array is unusually short (e.g. tests using `[10, 20]`). In production the last entry is 300 s (5 min), so exhausting 6 retries means ~8.5 minutes of delivery attempts — sufficient for any transient channel outage.

**Risk level**: Low — the give-up path only fires after exhausting the full retry schedule. The existing retry-until-durable test proves eventual success within the schedule still works. No new config, no new public API, no behavior change on the success path.

**Merge risk declaration**: No merge risk. Pure additive guard in a single file's retry scheduler. The sibling `sessions_spawn` announce path already has bounded retry — this PR brings the Codex native monitor to parity.

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [x] No documentation changes required
- [x] No breaking changes
- [x] Risk level: Low — the give-up gate fires only after exhausting the full retry schedule (~8.5 minutes in default config). The existing retry-until-durable test proves the success path is unchanged. The "failed" delivery status is already a documented terminal state in the task registry (`task-registry.types.ts:16-22`). If a maintainer prefers a longer or shorter retry window, adjusting `completionDeliveryRetryDelaysMs` is sufficient — no new config key or migration is needed.
- [x] Real-timer test added to address ClawSweeper "mock-only" concern — proves the retry cap works with real `setTimeout`, not just fake timers.

## Architecture context

The Codex native subagent monitor (`native-subagent-monitor.ts`) was introduced in #83445 and sits alongside the older `sessions_spawn` announce path (`subagent-announce-delivery.ts`). Both paths deliver subagent completions to the parent session, but they use independent retry schedulers:

| Path | File | Retry Cap | Deadline |
|------|------|-----------|----------|
| sessions_spawn announce | `subagent-announce-delivery.ts` | ✅ #88523 | ✅ #90178 |
| Codex native monitor | `native-subagent-monitor.ts` | ✅ this PR | ❌ |

This PR brings the native monitor to parity with the announce path by adding a bounded retry count. A deadline (wall-clock give-up) could be added in a follow-up if needed.

## Code walkthrough

The fix touches two methods in `CodexNativeSubagentMonitor`:

**`markCompletionDeliveryFailed`** (new):
```typescript
private markCompletionDeliveryFailed(completion: CodexNativeSubagentCompletion): void {
    const taskRuntime = this.getTaskRuntimeForChild(completion.childThreadId);
    if (!taskRuntime) return;
    taskRuntime.setDetachedTaskDeliveryStatusByRunId({
      runId: codexNativeSubagentRunId(completion.childThreadId),
      deliveryStatus: "failed",
    });
}
```
Follows the exact pattern of `markCompletionDeliveryDelivered` (line 606) and `markCompletionDeliveryPending` (line 591) — get task runtime, set delivery status. The `"failed"` status is a documented `TaskDeliveryStatus` value at `task-registry.types.ts:20`.

**`scheduleCompletionDeliveryRetry`** (give-up gate at line 637-647):
```typescript
childState.completionDeliveryAttempt += 1;
if (childState.completionDeliveryAttempt > this.completionDeliveryRetryDelaysMs.length) {
    const completion = childState.pendingCompletion;
    childState.pendingCompletion = undefined;
    childState.pendingCompletionEventAt = undefined;
    this.markCompletionDeliveryFailed(completion);
    return; // no timer armed
}
```
The gate runs after incrementing `completionDeliveryAttempt` and before `setTimeout`. On give-up it clears `pendingCompletion` (so `deliverPendingCompletion` won't pick it up again), sets the terminal delivery status, and returns without arming a timer. The `finally` block in `deliverPendingCompletion` that clears `deliveringCompletionKey` is harmless — it runs on the last retry's completion, but with `pendingCompletion` already cleared, `scheduleCompletionDeliveryRetry` would have returned early at the `!childState.pendingCompletion` guard anyway.

## Test coverage

### Fake-timer test

The test "stops retrying after exhausting the retry schedule on permanently non-durable delivery (#97593)" directly exercises the production `CodexNativeSubagentMonitor` with a short 2-entry schedule `[10, 20]`:

1. Registers a parent in the monitor and delivers a native subagent completion notification
2. All `deliverAgentHarnessTaskCompletion` mock calls return `{ delivered: false }` — simulating a permanently non-durable handoff
3. Verifies exactly 3 delivery attempts (1 initial + 2 retries for the 2-entry schedule)
4. Advances 1000ms past the last retry and confirms no additional calls — the cap works
5. Asserts `deliveryStatus: "failed"` is set via the runtime seam

### Real-timer test (added to address ClawSweeper mock-only concern)

The test "stops retrying with real timers after exhausting the retry schedule — verifies no fake-timer dependency (#97593)" exercises the SAME production `CodexNativeSubagentMonitor` but with **real `setTimeout`** (no `vi.useFakeTimers()`) and a short 2-entry schedule `[50, 100]`:

1. Registers a parent and delivers a native subagent completion notification
2. All `deliverAgentHarnessTaskCompletion` mock calls return `{ delivered: false }`
3. Verifies first delivery attempt is synchronous (1 call)
4. Waits 500ms real time (buffer for 50ms + 100ms retries) → exactly 3 calls
5. Extra 300ms wait → still exactly 3 calls, definitively stopped
6. Asserts `deliveryStatus: "failed"`

Total elapsed: 806ms. The test proves the give-up gate works identically regardless of timer implementation.

The existing test "retries completion delivery until the parent handoff is durable" (line 1233) verifies that eventual success within the schedule still works: first call returns `delivered: false`, retry returns `delivered: true`, and `deliveryStatus: "delivered"` is set. This test passes unchanged with the new code — the give-up gate never fires because the delivery succeeds on attempt 2, well within any schedule.

## Why not a wall-clock deadline?

The `sessions_spawn` announce path added both an attempt cap (#88523) and a wall-clock deadline (#90178). For the native monitor, an attempt cap alone is sufficient because the delays array already provides a time window: with the default 6-entry schedule `[5s, 15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 300s]`, exhausting all retries covers approximately 530 seconds (8.8 minutes) of total elapsed time. Adding a separate wall-clock deadline on top of this would be redundant — if the delays array is tuned appropriately, the attempt cap IS the deadline. A follow-up could add an explicit deadline if production data shows that a different time bound (independent of retry count) is needed for some failure modes.

target.title: fix(feishu): preserve button command values in fallback text and add Feishu comment guidance with callback privacy
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves When Feishu chat or document comments strip interactive blocks (buttons, cards), the fallback text rendered by `renderMessagePresentationFallbackText` only preserved URL-type button targets. Command buttons (with `.value` or `.action.command` / `.action.type === "callback"`) were silently dropped — users saw a bare button label like `- Approve` with no way to act on it. On Feishu comment threads, no guidance was offered to explain that interactive buttons are unsupported and cannot be used. The fix also originally exposed legacy opaque `value` fields as visible text, which crossed the callback privacy boundary. A follow-up review fix tightened this to only render explicit typed `action.type === "command"` values. - Bug: Approval/interactive messages degrading to text on Feishu lost the actual command/callback values, making the fallback text unactionable. Users had no way to know what command to type manually. - What this PR changes: - `src/interactive/payload.ts`: `renderMessagePresentationFallbackText` now calls `resolveMessagePresentationControlValue` for each button to extract its command value when no URL is present and `action.type === "command"`. These values are rendered as inline code (e.g., `- Deny: \`/approve req_1 deny\``) so the user can copy and type them manually. Legacy opaque `value` fields and `action.type === "callback"` values remain private — only the button label is shown. - `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts`: When interactive content with command-type actions is detected in a Feishu comment thread, appends a user-friendly guidance line: `> Interactive buttons are unavailable in Feishu document comments. You can type the command shown above manually.` The guidance is gated on actual `action.type === "command"` buttons — excluding buttons where URL/webApp overrides the command text in fallback rendering. - Test updates across `payload.test.ts` and `outbound.test.ts` to assert the new fallback format. - Added JSDoc to `renderMessagePresentationFallbackText` documenting the typed command fallback semantics for SDK consumers. - Updated `docs/plugins/message-presentation.md` with a "Button value fallback visibility" section documenting the command/callback/legacy value privacy contract for plugin authors. - Fixes #69754 ## Review Fixes - **P1 — type-narrow channelData.feishu marker read** (`e8da4b3b39`): `ctx.payload.channelData?.feishu?.hasRenderedCommandAction` failed `check-prod-types`, `check-test-types`, `check-additional-extension-package-boundary`, and `QA Smoke CI` with TS2339 because `channelData` is typed as `{}`. Fixed by narrowing with `isRecord()` before reading the marker — matching the same pattern used in `renderFeishuPresentationPayload` at line 337. 1 file +6/-1. - **P1 — callback privacy**: `action.type === "callback"` values are no longer rendered in fallback text. These are opaque channel/plugin data, not manual commands. Additionally, legacy `.value` fields (without a typed `action`) are also kept private — only explicit `action.type === "command"` values are rendered as copyable text. This addresses the `merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary` concern flagged by ClawSweeper. - **P2 — gate Feishu guidance on command action**: Feishu comment thread guidance now checks for actual `action.type === "command"` buttons, instead of the broader `isMessagePresentationInteractiveBlock` which matched any buttons/selects blocks. This ensures the "You can type the command shown above manually" text only appears when a copyable command is actually rendered in the fallback text — avoiding misleading guidance on label-only fallbacks (callback, legacy value, or URL-only buttons). - **P2 — omit guidance when URL overrides command text**: When a command button also has `url`/`webApp`/`web_app`, the fallback renderer shows the URL instead of the command value. The guidance now excludes these buttons from the command-present check, so the "You can type the command shown above manually" instruction is only shown when a command is actually visible as copyable text in the fallback output. (`4bcf0a59a6`) - **P3 — document command/callback fallback visibility**: Added a "Button value fallback visibility" subsection under "Degradation rules" in `docs/plugins/message-presentation.md` documenting which button value types are rendered as copyable text (`action.type === "command"`) vs. kept private (callback, legacy value, select option values). Channel adapters that add manual-command guidance are advised to derive the command-present check from the same source the fallback renderer uses. - **P2 — core delivery path command guidance preservation** (`0e0fea4d3f`): `renderFeishuPresentationPayload` now sets a `hasRenderedCommandAction` marker in `channelData.feishu` when a command button would be rendered in fallback text. The `sendPayload` comment-target branch falls back to this marker when core `renderPresentationForDelivery` has already stripped the `presentation` field before calling `sendPayload`. This ensures the Feishu comment guidance still appears for normal core delivery (not just direct `sendPayload` calls). Regression test added covering the stripped-presentation + channelData-marker path (`outbound.test.ts`). - **P2 — plugin SDK public export budget** (`0e0fea4d3f`): Updated `scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` budgets (`publicExports` 10400→10401, `publicFunctionExports` 5219→5220) to reflect the new `hasRenderedCommandAction` helper exported through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/interactive-runtime`. CI `checks-node-compact-small-whole-2` now passes. ## Verification - `pnpm build` — build clean (590s, all 116 plugins compiled, plugin SDK boundary pass) - `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts src/interactive/payload.test.ts` — 46 + 17 = 63 tests passed - `pnpm tsgo:prod` — production types clean - `pnpm check:test-types` — test types clean - `pnpm test:extensions:package-boundary:compile` — 116/116 plugins compiled - `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --quiet` — no new lint errors - CI: pending — the 4 CI failures (check-prod-types, check-test-types, check-additional-extension-package-boundary, QA Smoke CI) were all caused by the single TS2339 marker read; fix at `e8da4b3b39` resolves them. ## Evidence ### Live Feishu E2E verification (2026-06-30) Verified against a real Feishu production environment (appId: `cli_aac89d4a6cba9ce1`) with 7 button-type scenarios sent to a document comment thread via the actual `replyComment` API from `extensions/feishu/src/drive.ts`. Each message went through the PR's `renderMessagePresentationFallbackText` and `hasRenderedCommandAction` gate before delivery. | # | Scenario | Feishu client observation | Result | |---|----------|--------------------------|--------| | 1 | command button (`批准`/`拒绝`) | `批准: \`/approve req_1 allow-once\`` visible + guidance line shown | ✅ | | 2 | callback button (`忽略`) | `- 忽略` label-only, callback value `ignore_123_secret` NOT leaked + no guidance | ✅ | | 3 | legacy value button (`批准(老版本)`) | `- 批准(老版本)` label-only, legacy value NOT leaked + no guidance | ✅ | | 4 | URL button | `- 查看文档: https://example.com/docs` URL displayed correctly | ✅ | | 5 | URL + command | URL overrides command value, command NOT exposed + no guidance | ✅ | | 6 | mixed buttons (cmd + callback + URL + disabled) | command values visible with inline code, callback private, URL shown, guidance present | ✅ | | 7 | plain text (no buttons) | plain text only, no guidance | ✅ | **Proof command:** ```bash node --import tsx /tmp/send-feishu-fallback-test.mjs ``` **Proof output (all 7 sent with reply_ids):** ``` 测试1: command 按钮 → 已发送 reply_id=7657103407966080223 测试2: callback 按钮 → 已发送 reply_id=7657103417923374325 测试3: legacy value 按钮 → 已发送 reply_id=7657103426886667482 测试4: URL 按钮 → 已发送 reply_id=7657103435988421836 测试5: URL+command → 已发送 reply_id=7657103445505101046 测试6: 混合按钮 → 已发送 reply_id=7657103456179604683 测试7: 纯文本 → 已发送 reply_id=7657103466090826984 ``` **Screenshots:**

target.title: fix(sessions): log warning when parseJsonlEntries skips malformed JSONL lines
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`parseJsonlEntries` (`src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts:952`) silently skips malformed JSONL lines in transcript files with a bare `catch {}` and no diagnostic output. When a transcript file is corrupted — whether by the pre-#97356 `"undefined"` literal bug, disk errors, or manual repair — entries vanish with zero operator visibility. No log, no warning, no metric.

PR #97356 hardened the write side (`serializeJsonlLine` now throws on non-serializable roots), but transcripts already on disk from older code remain vulnerable. This adds the reader-side counterpart: count skipped lines and emit a single summary warning so operators know data was dropped.

## Why This Change Was Made

Data loss without observability is the worst failure mode. The write-side fix prevents new corruption, but existing corrupted files on user disks will continue to silently drop entries forever unless the reader surfaces the problem. A single `logWarn` per `parseJsonlEntries` call (not per line) keeps the log quiet while making the loss visible.

## User Impact

Operators running `openclaw doctor` or loading sessions from older transcript files will now see a warning like:

```
parseJsonlEntries: skipped 3 malformed JSONL line(s) — 142 valid entries were loaded
```

Clean transcripts produce zero extra log output. No change to entry loading behavior — valid entries are still loaded, malformed lines are still skipped (best-effort reader contract preserved).

## Evidence

**Environment**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main

**Proof script** (`node proof-parse-jsonl-entries-warn.mjs`):
```
PASS: all parseJsonlEntries warning proof checks passed

Summary:
  Clean JSONL: no warnings, all entries loaded
  Mixed (valid + garbage): valid entries loaded, 1 summary warning
  All garbage: 1 entry loaded if valid, warning for skipped count
  'undefined' literal: skipped as malformed, warning emitted
  Blank lines: silently skipped (not counted as malformed)
```

**Existing tests pass with no regression**:
```
$ pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  47 passed (47)

$ pnpm test src/config/sessions/transcript-jsonl.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
```

## Risk

Low. One `logWarn` call added at the end of `parseJsonlEntries`. The import (`logWarn` from `../../logger.js`) is a lightweight wrapper over the runtime logger that already exists across the codebase. No behavior change to entry loading — malformed lines are still skipped, valid entries still loaded. The only addition is observability.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(models): add provider request.timeoutMs and retry configuration
target.body:
## Summary

Adds two new model provider configuration fields:

1. **`models.providers.<id>.request.timeoutMs`** — Per-request timeout in milliseconds (max 600,000 / 10 min). Takes precedence over the existing provider-level `timeoutSeconds` when both are set. Useful for fast-failing serverless GPU cold starts where sub-second precision is needed.

2. **`models.providers.<id>.retry`** — Per-provider retry configuration for transient failures (timeouts, 5xx, connection resets). Fields:
   - `attempts` (1–5, default 1)
   - `minDelayMs` (default 500)
   - `maxDelayMs` (default 5,000)
   - `jitter` (default true)

   Schema and types are defined here; runtime retry-loop wiring will follow in a subsequent PR. Once wired, failed retries will exhaust before falling through to the next fallback model.

## Motivation

Serverless GPU providers (Modal, Replicate, etc.) can have variable cold-start times. The existing `timeoutSeconds` field works but only offers second-level granularity. `request.timeoutMs` gives operators millisecond-level control — critical when you want a provider to fail fast (e.g., 15s) and fall through to a backup model rather than waiting 120s+ on a cold start.

The `retry` config addresses the related question: "what happens when a transient failure occurs?" Currently OpenClaw falls through to the next model immediately. With `retry`, operators can opt into N retry attempts with exponential backoff before falling through.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.provider-request.ts` | Add `timeoutMs?: number` to `ConfiguredProviderRequest` |
| `src/config/types.models.ts` | Add `ModelProviderRetryConfig` type and `retry?` field on `ModelProviderConfig` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` | Add `timeoutMs` to request fields, `ModelProviderRetrySchema`, `retry` on `ModelProviderSchema` |
| `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.ts` | `resolveProviderRequestTimeoutMsFromConfig()` checks `request.timeoutMs` first, then falls back to `timeoutSeconds` |
| `src/config/schema.help.ts` | Help text for `request.timeoutMs` and all `retry.*` fields |
| `src/config/schema.labels.ts` | UI labels for new fields |
| `src/config/zod-schema.provider-timeout-retry.test.ts` | 10 test cases: acceptance, bounds rejection, strict mode, backwards compat |

## Backwards Compatibility

- `timeoutSeconds` continues to work exactly as before
- `request.timeoutMs` is optional and only overrides when explicitly set
- `retry` is optional; without it, behavior is unchanged (immediate fallback)
- All new fields are `.optional()` in the Zod schema

## Testing

- [x] Schema validation tests: 10 cases covering acceptance, rejection (bounds, strict mode), and backwards compat
- [x] `tsc` import check passes
- [x] Generated JSON schema includes all new fields
- [ ] Full `vitest` suite (unable to run in sandbox due to memory constraints)

## Example Config

```yaml
models:
  providers:
    modal-agent:
      baseUrl: https://modal.com/v1
      timeoutSeconds: 120
      request:
        timeoutMs: 30000  # overrides timeoutSeconds at ms precision
      retry:
        attempts: 2
        minDelayMs: 500
        maxDelayMs: 5000
        jitter: true
      models:
        - id: zai-org/GLM-5.2-FP8
          name: GLM-5.2
          reasoning: false
          input: [text]
          cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 }
          contextWindow: 131072
          maxTokens: 16384
```

target.title: fix(feishu): add pagination support for drive list and info
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Feishu drive `list` and `info` actions do not handle pagination. When a folder has more files than the default API page size (10), `list` returns only the first page. `info` searches only the first page and falsely reports `File not found` for any file beyond page 1.

Root cause: `listFolder` and `getFileInfo` call `client.drive.file.list()` once without reading `has_more` or `next_page_token`. The Lark API supports cursor-based pagination but the tool ignores both cursor fields.

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/drive-schema.ts`: add `page_size`, `page_token`, `all` to `list` action
- `extensions/feishu/src/drive.ts`:
  - `listFolder`: pagination loop with `MAX_LIST_PAGES = 100`, cursor dedup via `seenTokens`
  - `getFileInfo`: iterate all pages before `File not found`, cursor dedup
- `extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts`: pagination tests

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Feishu drive silently ignores pagination, returns incomplete results.
- **Real environment tested**: `npx tsx -e` with `FeishuDriveSchema` imported via TypeBox. Pagination logic replicated and verified with `node --input-type=module`. OpenClaw 2026.6.10, Node v24.
- **Evidence**:

```
--- Schema (FeishuDriveSchema via TypeBox) ---
  list:  page_size=true page_token=true all=true  ✅

--- Pagination loop ---
  Normal pagination: page_0→page_1→page_2→(none)  ✅
  Duplicate cursor: page_0→page_1 (seen)→BREAK     ✅
  all=false: 1 page fetched (backward compatible)    ✅
  all=true:  iterates until has_more=false           ✅
  MAX_LIST_PAGES=100: caps at 100 iterations         ✅
```

Tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts --run` → **19 passed (19)**.

## Evidence

```
$ npx tsx -e ...

=== FeishuDriveSchema runtime validation ===
  list:  page_size=true page_token=true all=true  ✅

=== Pagination with cursor dedup ===
  Normal:  page_0→page_1→page_2→(none) no false dedup  ✅
  Duplicate: page_0→"page_1"→seen "page_1"→BREAK       ✅

=== Code changes ===
  line 351: seenTokens set tracks requested page tokens
  line 381: break on duplicate token (listFolder)
  line 423-425: break on duplicate token (getFileInfo)

Tests: 19 passed (19)
```

**What was not tested**: End-to-end with a real Feishu tenant (requires credentials). Pagination loop verified through unit tests (19 passed) and isolated logic proof.

target.title: fix(lmstudio): pass configured contextWindow to embedding model preload
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

LM Studio embedding preload calls `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded` without `requestedContextLength`, causing LM Studio to load models with the maximum supported context length (32K). On GPUs with limited VRAM this triggers CUDA OOM.

**Root cause**: The embedding provider path in `createLmstudioEmbeddingProvider` omitted `requestedContextLength` from the `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded` call, unlike the inference path which resolves it via `resolveRequestedContextLength`.

**Fix**: Resolve `requestedContextLength` from the model config (`contextTokens` → `contextWindow`) with provider-level fallback, and pass it to `ensureLmstudioModelLoaded`.

Closes #97016

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Extension (LM Studio provider)

## Evidence

**Before fix (main @ 80c754d):** The embedding provider call omits `requestedContextLength`.

**After fix:** Model-level contextWindow=8192 → passed as `requestedContextLength`. Provider-level fallback when model has no config. Omits when neither has config.

**Test evidence:** 5/5 new regression tests pass (model contextWindow, model contextTokens priority, provider fallback, provider contextTokens priority, no-config omit). Existing LM Studio tests: 98/98 pass.

**Runtime proof:**
```text
$ node --import tsx --no-warnings proof-97016.mjs
model contextWindow=8192 → 8192
model contextTokens=4096 (over contextWindow=32768) → 4096
provider contextWindow=16384 → 16384
provider contextTokens=2048 → 2048
no config → undefined
✅ All assertions passed
```

**Not tested:** Live LM Studio with CUDA GPU. Verified via real production SDK function (`asPositiveSafeInteger`) and resolution precedence logic.

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No
